RSK Ireland Geo \- Environmental Team are now hiring for a **Graduate Geo\-Environmental Consultant** to join the team in **Dublin** . This is an excellent opportunity to work alongside a highly experienced team and a chance to get involved with a vast array of projects ranging from large scale brownfield site development, landfill investigations, contaminated site investigations, renewable energy projects and hydrogeology specific projects. **Key Responsibilities:** * Undertaking Preliminary Risk Assessments / Preliminary site Assessments (Phase 1 Desk Studies) and intrusive site investigations for environmental purposes * Soil and rock logging to current standards * Groundwater and surface water sampling * Managing fieldwork activities and subcontractors * Supporting and co\-ordinating a variety of projects * Preparing health and safety documentation, booking subcontractors and scheduling soil and water samples for analytical and geotechnical testing **Person Specification:** * BSc degree level qualified in Geology or a related Environmental subject * MSc in Engineering Geology/Engineering or Contaminated Land related subjects preferable but not essential * Previous site experience would be an advantage. * Interest in site investigations techniques, basic contaminated land assessment. * Understanding of laboratory testing and interpreting results with working knowledge of geotechnical and environmental guidance. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * Prepared to work as part of a team or as an individual * Candidates must be willing to spend a significant amount of their working day out on site and away from home. * Full driving licence and access to a car. **Salary \& Benefits:** * Competitive salary * Continual professional development. Both internally and sponsored by RSK where required / relevant. * A flexible benefits programme including the option to buy additional holidays \#GraduateGeoenvironmental \#Geology \#Geoscience **RSK Group is an equal opportunities employer.** **The Company** RSK is a global leader in sustainable solutions focusing on environmental, engineering, and technical services. Working together, we address societal challenges aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, offering integrated solutions across sectors such as water, energy, food, infrastructure, cities, mining, and waste management. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that welcomes individuals from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We firmly believe that embracing and celebrating the diversity in our workforce makes us a stronger and more innovative company. By providing workplace adjustments, we make it easier for people to confidently explore and pursue our career opportunities and recognise that flexibility is key to fostering a dynamic and thriving workplace. Many of our office\-based positions benefit from a flexible working arrangement, allowing our team members to achieve a balance between professional and personal commitments. We actively encourage applications from candidates of all genders, races, ethnicities, ages, neurotypes, religions or beliefs, sexual orientations, and those with disabilities. Since the beginning, our philosophy has remained the same: employ, develop and reward talented individuals. People are crucial to our company, and we recognise that our success hinges on our colleagues' growth. We're committed to preserving RSK's people\-first culture, promoting innovation, and fostering a supportive and caring environment for collaboration. At RSK, we want you to be part of our successes and help you reach your career aspirations. We take pride in offering employees fulfilling and challenging careers by promoting ongoing growth and empowering individuals to excel. If you're passionate about making a difference, join us as we work together to build a brighter, more equitable, and sustainable future for all. Do you have what it takes?

title: HR & Business Operations Graduate
description: **Protex AI is the AI Safety and Operations Intelligence Company.** At Protex AI, we are at the forefront of AI\-driven computer vision, building a safer, smarter industrial workplace with an intelligent operating system that redefines how facilities operate. Backed by top\-tier global investors, we recently secured a $36 million Series B to accelerate our mission. Industry leaders like DHL, Amazon, and Tesla trust Protex AI to drive measurable safety improvements, achieving an average 64% risk reduction within just three months of deployment. Operating in 20\+ countries, Protex is the go\-to safety partner for Fortune 500 manufacturing and logistics enterprises, transforming workplace safety with real\-time, AI\-powered insights. This is not a typical graduate role. As we scale globally, you will sit at the very centre of the business , working across People, Operations, Facilities, Finance, and Legal to keep a fast\-moving, international company running smoothly. You will support teams and stakeholders across multiple geographies, helping to build and maintain the operational foundations that allow a growing global company to move quickly and effectively. No two days will look the same, and that is exactly the point. We are a company that embraces technology and AI as a core part of how we operate. You will be expected to use AI tools confidently, whether drafting documents, researching suppliers or automating repetitive tasks. If you already experiment with AI in your daily life, you will fit right in. **KEY RESPONSIBILITIES** 1\. PEOPLE \& HR SUPPORT * Support the People team with onboarding coordination — contracts, starter packs, and IT setup requests — across multiple regions * Help maintain accurate employee records, org charts, and HR documentation as the company scales * Assist with scheduling and logistics for interviews, performance review cycles, and team events globally * Support the coordination of employee engagement initiatives, company communications , surveys, and culture programmes * Act as a first point of contact for general People queries, escalating where appropriate 2\. BUSINESS OPERATIONS * Support the smooth day\-to\-day running of business operations across a growing, international organisation * Help maintain operational trackers, dashboards, and process documentation as we scale into new markets * Assist with cross\-functional projects — from research and data gathering to drafting updates and presentations * Use AI tools proactively to streamline workflows, automate repetitive tasks, and improve how we work * Manage internal communications and follow\-ups to ensure actions are captured and progressed 3\. OFFICE MANAGEMENT \& FACILITIES * Oversee the day\-to\-day running of the office environment — supplies, equipment, and vendor relationships * Act as the primary point of contact for facilities queries, maintenance requests, and health \& safety compliance * Manage meeting room bookings, visitor coordination, and office access * Support planning and logistics for company events, offsites, and global town halls * Own the relationship with building management and key service providers 4\. FINANCE SUPPORT * Assist with purchase order (PO) creation, invoice processing, and expense management * Support month\-end processes including cost tracking and basic budget reconciliation * Help maintain financial records and filing in line with company policy * Liaise with the Finance team on approvals and payment queries from internal stakeholders across regions 5\. CO\-FOUNDER SUPPORT * Provide diary management support for the Co\-Founders — scheduling meetings, coordinating international travel where required * Handle ad hoc personal and business requests with discretion and professionalism **THE GRADUATE WE'RE LOOKING FOR** ESSENTIAL * Degree\-educated (any discipline), with a strong academic record or demonstrable aptitude * Genuinely enthusiastic — brings positive energy and curiosity every single day * Comfortable with technology and actively uses AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Notion AI, or similar) in everyday work or life * Highly organised with exceptional attention to detail — nothing falls through the cracks * A natural multi\-tasker who is comfortable switching between very different priorities at pace * Strong written and verbal communication skills — clear, concise, and professional across cultures * Proactive and self\-motivated — identifies what needs doing and gets on with it * Discreet and trustworthy when handling sensitive or confidential information * Comfortable with ambiguity; thrives when given autonomy rather than a rigid brief * Fast learner who asks good questions and is not afraid to figure things out DESIRABLE * Previous internship or work experience in a fast\-growth startup, professional services, or operations environment * Exposure to HR systems (e.g. BambooHR, Rippling, HiBob) or project management tools (e.g. Notion, Asana, Monday.com) * Has built personal workflows or automations using AI tools (e.g. Zapier, Make, or AI\-assisted drafting) * Interest in finance, operations, or people functions as a long\-term career pathway * Experience working in or supporting an international or multi\-site organisation * Experience providing administrative or coordination support to senior stakeholders TECH \& AI COMFORT — WHAT WE MEAN You don't need to be a developer. But you should be genuinely curious about technology and comfortable picking up new tools quickly. Familiarity with AI tools is a strong plus — and we will support you in building expertise further. **WHAT WE OFFER** * Compensation package of €35,000 per year, as well as benefits * A front\-row seat at a fast\-growing global company — your work will matter from day one * Real breadth of exposure across People, Operations, Finance, Legal, and the Founders * The chance to help build and shape operational processes as we scale internationally * Direct access to experienced founders and senior leadership * Competitive graduate salary with clear progression pathways * A culture that invests in AI tools, learning, and doing things smarter * Hybrid working and a vibrant, collaborative office environment * Regular company events, team socials, and a strong people\-first culture **KEY COMPETENCIES** Candidates will be assessed against the following core competencies throughout the hiring process: Adaptability \& Flexibility Comfortable moving between very different tasks — from diary management to cross\-border compliance filing — with equal care and quality. Stakeholder Management Builds positive relationships at all levels and across cultures, from office suppliers to Co\-Founders, and communicates effectively in all directions. Ownership \& Initiative Takes responsibility for outcomes rather than waiting to be directed. Proactively flags risks and proposes solutions. Rigour \& Accuracy Produces accurate, well\-organised work consistently — especially critical when supporting finance, legal, and people functions across geographies. Discretion \& Integrity Handles confidential information with maturity and sound judgement at all times. Drive \& Ambition Brings energy, enthusiasm, and a genuine desire to grow. Sees this role as a launchpad and is motivated to develop across multiple disciplines in a global environment. **SOUND LIKE YOU?** This role is deliberately broad — and the right person will be energised by that, not daunted. We want a bright, enthusiastic graduate who loves technology, moves fast, and takes real pride in making things run smoothly across a growing global business. Someone who is just as happy sorting out the office as they are chasing down a contract signature, or helping onboard a new joiner in another country. title: HR & Business Operations Graduate
description: **Protex AI is the AI Safety and Operations Intelligence Company.** At Protex AI, we are at the forefront of AI\-driven computer vision, building a safer, smarter industrial workplace with an intelligent operating system that redefines how facilities operate. Backed by top\-tier global investors, we recently secured a $36 million Series B to accelerate our mission. Industry leaders like DHL, Amazon, and Tesla trust Protex AI to drive measurable safety improvements, achieving an average 64% risk reduction within just three months of deployment. Operating in 20\+ countries, Protex is the go\-to safety partner for Fortune 500 manufacturing and logistics enterprises, transforming workplace safety with real\-time, AI\-powered insights. This is not a typical graduate role. As we scale globally, you will sit at the very centre of the business , working across People, Operations, Facilities, Finance, and Legal to keep a fast\-moving, international company running smoothly. You will support teams and stakeholders across multiple geographies, helping to build and maintain the operational foundations that allow a growing global company to move quickly and effectively. No two days will look the same, and that is exactly the point. We are a company that embraces technology and AI as a core part of how we operate. You will be expected to use AI tools confidently, whether drafting documents, researching suppliers or automating repetitive tasks. If you already experiment with AI in your daily life, you will fit right in. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ **Key Responsibilities** * PEOPLE \& HR SUPPORT * Support the People team with onboarding coordination — contracts, starter packs, and IT setup requests — across multiple regions * Help maintain accurate employee records, org charts, and HR documentation as the company scales * Assist with scheduling and logistics for interviews, performance review cycles, and team events globally * Support the coordination of employee engagement initiatives, company communications , surveys, and culture programmes * Act as a first point of contact for general People queries, escalating where appropriate * BUSINESS OPERATIONS * Support the smooth day\-to\-day running of business operations across a growing, international organisation * Help maintain operational trackers, dashboards, and process documentation as we scale into new markets * Assist with cross\-functional projects — from research and data gathering to drafting updates and presentations * Use AI tools proactively to streamline workflows, automate repetitive tasks, and improve how we work * Manage internal communications and follow\-ups to ensure actions are captured and progressed * OFFICE MANAGEMENT \& FACILITIES * Oversee the day\-to\-day running of the office environment — supplies, equipment, and vendor relationships * Act as the primary point of contact for facilities queries, maintenance requests, and health \& safety compliance * Manage meeting room bookings, visitor coordination, and office access * Support planning and logistics for company events, offsites, and global town halls * Own the relationship with building management and key service providers * FINANCE SUPPORT * Assist with purchase order (PO) creation, invoice processing, and expense management * Support month\-end processes including cost tracking and basic budget reconciliation * Help maintain financial records and filing in line with company policy * Liaise with the Finance team on approvals and payment queries from internal stakeholders across regions * CO\-FOUNDER SUPPORT * Provide diary management support for the Co\-Founders — scheduling meetings, coordinating international travel where required * Handle ad hoc personal and business requests with discretion and professionalism ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ **THE GRADUATE WE'RE LOOKING FOR** **Essential** * Degree\-educated (any discipline), with a strong academic record or demonstrable aptitude * Genuinely enthusiastic — brings positive energy and curiosity every single day * Comfortable with technology and actively uses AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Notion AI, or similar) in everyday work or life * Highly organised with exceptional attention to detail — nothing falls through the cracks * A natural multi\-tasker who is comfortable switching between very different priorities at pace * Strong written and verbal communication skills — clear, concise, and professional across cultures * Proactive and self\-motivated — identifies what needs doing and gets on with it * Discreet and trustworthy when handling sensitive or confidential information * Comfortable with ambiguity; thrives when given autonomy rather than a rigid brief * Fast learner who asks good questions and is not afraid to figure things out DESIRABLE * Previous internship or work experience in a fast\-growth startup, professional services, or operations environment * Exposure to HR systems (e.g. BambooHR, Rippling, HiBob) or project management tools (e.g. Notion, Asana, Monday.com) * Has built personal workflows or automations using AI tools (e.g. Zapier, Make, or AI\-assisted drafting) * Interest in finance, operations, or people functions as a long\-term career pathway * Experience working in or supporting an international or multi\-site organisation * Experience providing administrative or coordination support to senior stakeholders **Tech \& Ai Comfort — What We Mean** You don't need to be a developer. But you should be genuinely curious about technology and comfortable picking up new tools quickly. Familiarity with AI tools is a strong plus — and we will support you in building expertise further. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ **What We Offer** * Compensation package of €35,000 per year, as well as benefits * A front\-row seat at a fast\-growing global company — your work will matter from day one * Real breadth of exposure across People, Operations, Finance, Legal, and the Founders * The chance to help build and shape operational processes as we scale internationally * Direct access to experienced founders and senior leadership * Competitive graduate salary with clear progression pathways * A culture that invests in AI tools, learning, and doing things smarter * Hybrid working and a vibrant, collaborative office environment * Regular company events, team socials, and a strong people\-first culture ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ **KEY COMPETENCIES** Candidates will be assessed against the following core competencies throughout the hiring process: Adaptability \& Flexibility Comfortable moving between very different tasks — from diary management to cross\-border compliance filing — with equal care and quality. Stakeholder Management Builds positive relationships at all levels and across cultures, from office suppliers to Co\-Founders, and communicates effectively in all directions. Ownership \& Initiative Takes responsibility for outcomes rather than waiting to be directed. Proactively flags risks and proposes solutions. Rigour \& Accuracy Produces accurate, well\-organised work consistently — especially critical when supporting finance, legal, and people functions across geographies. Discretion \& Integrity Handles confidential information with maturity and sound judgement at all times. Drive \& Ambition Brings energy, enthusiasm, and a genuine desire to grow. Sees this role as a launchpad and is motivated to develop across multiple disciplines in a global environment. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ **SOUND LIKE YOU?** This role is deliberately broad — and the right person will be energised by that, not daunted. We want a bright, enthusiastic graduate who loves technology, moves fast, and takes real pride in making things run smoothly across a growing global business. Someone who is just as happy sorting out the office as they are chasing down a contract signature, or helping onboard a new joiner in another country. If that sounds like you — we'd love to hear from you. Protex AI is an inclusive and equal opportunities employer. We are committed to creating an equitable workplace for everyone regardless of gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, education level, or race.

title: HR Operations Lifecycle Specialist - EMEA - Ireland
description: **Responsibilities** The HR operations team, endeavours to continuously build and deliver a seamless and positive employee experience across the whole employee lifecycle. We are dedicated to leading with care and have empathy in mind. We strive to develop efficient and simple people processes, systems, policies, and programs. We aspire to ensure employees feel heard and that they are given the appropriate resources and support needed to be effective and efficient during their careers. Responsibilities: 1\. Ensure legal compliance by managing and implementing applicable human resource local requirements for the local market in Ireland, with the potential of extended remit to additional countries within Europe. 2\. Be responsible for vendor management to improve operation and service quality, efficiency and experience (e.g. benefits vendors). 3\. Committed to building good and harmonious employee relations. 4\. First point of contact for any employee queries. Support employees and improve employee experience by providing end\-to\-end HR solutions to solve their issues. 5\. Design and recommend enhancements, improvements and simplifications of existing HR processes and procedures. 6\. Support employee life cycle management processes such as on\-boarding management, termination, personal data changes, internal transfers , leave management \& benefit operations including relevant documentation and employee file management in accordance to local legislation. 7\. Support relocation, immigration and other responsibilities related to staffing administration. **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications: 1\. Relevant training/degree and experience within People Operations/Human Resources. 2\. Strong knowledge of UK \& Ireland employment legislation/ compliance laws and market practices and favourably additional knowledge of other countries. 3\. Experience with employee lifecycle (onboarding, transfers, performance cycles, leaves of absence and off\-boarding) and providing end to end HR solutions. 4\. Analytical and problem\-solving skills. 5\. High level of discretion, confidentiality, and ethics. 6\. Humility, flexibility, and good interpersonal \& customer facing skills are a must. 7\. Excellent communication skills. Preferred Qualifications: 1\. Strong knowledge of multiple legislations and market practices within the region. **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​ **Why Join Us** Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day.​ We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​ ​ Diversity \& Inclusion​ TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​

title: IDC Facility Engineer
description: IDC Facility Engineer Ireland Level L1\-L2 / On\-site Facility Operations Support Function Critical Facility Systems, Electrical / Cooling / Environmental Operations Job Summary We are looking for an IDC Facility Engineer to support the daily operation and maintenance of data center facility systems in Finland and Ireland. The role focuses on stable operation of critical infrastructure, including electrical systems, power distribution, UPS, generators, cooling, ventilation, and environmental monitoring. This position is suitable for candidates with hands\-on experience in data center facilities, critical environments, electrical systems, HVAC, industrial maintenance, or building technical operations. The role starts from on\-site operational support and may grow into broader facility coordination or shift lead responsibilities depending on project needs and individual capability. Key Responsibilities * Support daily inspection, monitoring, and operation of data center facility systems, including power supply, UPS, generators, cooling, ventilation, and environmental control systems. * Follow standard operating procedures for routine checks, alarm response, escalation, and incident reporting. * Support preventive maintenance activities and coordinate with internal teams, vendors, and subcontractors when required. * Identify facility risks, abnormalities, or potential failures, and report them promptly according to escalation procedures. * Support first\-level troubleshooting for facility\-related issues under the guidance of senior engineers or technical leads. * Maintain accurate operation records, inspection logs, maintenance reports, and incident documentation. * Ensure all activities are carried out in compliance with site safety rules, customer requirements, and internal operational standards. * Cooperate with IT operations, asset management, security, and project delivery teams to ensure smooth daily data center operations. Requirements * Previous experience in data center facilities, critical facilities, electrical systems, HVAC, industrial maintenance, or building technical operations is preferred. * Basic understanding of electrical distribution, UPS, generators, cooling systems, or environmental monitoring systems. * Ability to follow SOPs, checklists, permit\-to\-work requirements, and safety procedures carefully. * Good sense of responsibility, safety awareness, and attention to detail. * Able to work in an on\-site data center environment and support shift work if required. * Good communication skills in English. Finnish or local language skills are an advantage depending on site needs. * Relevant technical education or certification in electrical, mechanical, HVAC, automation, facility management, or related fields is preferred. What We Value * Strong safety awareness, compliance mindset, and respect for procedures. * Hands\-on attitude and willingness to work in an on\-site technical environment. * Reliability, punctuality, ownership, and attention to detail. * Clear communication and timely escalation when issues or risks appear. * Teamwork across IT operations, facility, security, asset management, vendors, and customer\-side teams. * A learning mindset. Candidates do not need to know everything from day one, but they must be able to follow SOPs carefully and grow with the project. What We Provide * A role in international data center operations, with exposure to stable, process\-driven IDC delivery in Europe. * Role\-specific onboarding, site induction, SOP training, and support from experienced technical and project teams. * Necessary work equipment, tools, PPE, and site access support according to project and site requirements. 职位类型：全职 Work Location: 现场办公 职位类型：全职 Work Location: 现场办公

title: IT Programming Teacher - FET
company: Kilkenny And Carlow Education And Training Board
description: ***Teaching and learning is the core focus of Kilkenny and Carlow ETB and we are committed to recruiting high quality teachers with appropriate qualifications, expertise and experience.*** **Qualifications** * Applicants must meet the general and special requirements as set out from time to time by the Teaching Council of Ireland for the advertised subject(s). Further details and a full list of recognised qualifications are available on www.teachingcouncil.ie. * Applicants who are in their final year of degree/postgraduate diploma which will lead to a teaching qualification, but who have not yet completed their final exams and/or received their results, may apply for advertised vacancies, subject to the provisions regarding Teaching Council registration set out below. Any offer of interview or employment is made pending the applicant’s results meeting the requirements of the vacancy. **Registration with the Teaching Council of Ireland (Essential)** * Applicants must be currently registered with the Teaching Council of Ireland. * Applicants shall provide a current Registration Certificate, or confirmation of Registration with their Application Form for a teaching position. **Provisional Interview Date:** week commence 10th or 17 August 2026\. **Teaching****Competences** Persons appointed to teaching positions within Kilkenny and Carlow ETB will be required to show evidence of the following key competences: * + Professional Knowledge + Professional Practice + Communication and relationship building + Self\-awareness and self\-management skills Candidates will be required to describe in no more than 300 words per competency some of their personal achievements to\-date that demonstrate the key competencies required for the position as outlined. Candidates should describe situation/s from their own experience, which they think are the best example/s of what they have done which demonstrates the specific competencies. The example(s) may be drawn from candidates experience in various settings including their professional, community or voluntary involvement. **Professional Knowledge** *Demonstrate in\-depth knowledge, comprehension and understanding of both the subject discipline and pedagogy* *Indicators include:* * Has a strong knowledge of subject matter. * Has an awareness and understanding of specific requirements of course/syllabus/specifications. * Keeps up to date with subject matter/course syllabus changes and developments in the education system. * Understands the cross\-curricular links to other content areas and is able to integrate learning across and between content areas (e.g. literacy, numeracy, critical thinking). * Knows, understands and critically evaluates the range of teaching and learning philosophies / theories and knows how to apply them appropriately within their subject (s). * Is aware of the range of supports available to support curriculum development and implementation. * Participates in and contributes to educational planning and development within the school e.g. lesson planning, subject department planning, etc. **Professional Practice** *Understands the nature of high quality teaching and learning and demonstrates the skills and competencies to illustrate these.* *Indicators include:* ***Planning and Preparation*** * Plans and communicates clear, challenging, appropriate and achievable expectations for students. * Shows knowledge and understanding of how to structure and schedule schemes of work, class plans, subject department plans etc. * Knows and understands the relationship between the planning, implementation and evaluation of the curriculum. * Selects, creates and pitches class materials which respond to the needs of students. **Teaching and Learning** * Understands the factors that contribute to a stimulating learning environment and the role of the teacher in supporting this environment. * Understands the practices that underpin effective classroom teaching and meet the learning styles of a diverse range of students e.g. differentiation, learning intentions, group work, etc. * Establishes standards of behaviour that enable all students to learn and deals with inappropriate behaviour in the context of school policies. * Demonstrates in\-depth knowledge and understanding of classroom management techniques and skills. * Effectively manages the range of behaviours/situations that can occur in the classroom in line with School Code of Behaviour maintaining good order and discipline among students. * Effectively uses a variety of teaching materials and technologies to actively engage students in the learning process e.g. collaboration, technology enhanced teaching and learning. ***Assessment*** * Focuses on assessment for learning by monitoring student progress, giving constructive feedback and helping students to reflect on and improve their learning e.g. formative and summative assessment. * Selects from a range of assessment strategies to evaluate student learning, and uses this information in their planning to help make their teaching more effective. * Assesses the levels of student attainment against relevant benchmarking data and understands the relationship between student assessment and target setting. * Liaises orally and in written reports in an effective manner with parents/guardians on student progress and achievement. **Communication and relationship building** *Establishes effective communication and engagement to ensure positive and effective working relationships with management, colleagues, students, parents and the wider school community.* *Indicators include:* * Seeks to foster positive relationships with students, colleagues, parents, school management and the wider school community in a professional, collaborative, supportive and respectful manner. * Communicates and contributes effectively and collaboratively within the school team environment. * Shows the capacity and skills to relate to and communicate effectively with students, parents, colleagues, school management and the wider school community. * Supports conflict resolution in all aspects of teaching role. * Motivates, inspires and celebrates student effort and success. * Is committed to equality and inclusion and to respecting and accommodating diversity. * Understands how to exercise a duty of care to students. * Participates in and supports the extra\-curricular programmes/activities of the school. * Contributes positively to the school community. **Self\-awareness and self\-management skills** *Is self\-aware and has the capacity to self\-manage and develop personally and professionally*. *Indicators include:* * Engages in reflective practice of their own work and consistently reviews it in order to ensure it is of the highest possible standard and responsive to changing teaching demands. * Maintains a strong focus on personal and professional growth including continual professional development, participation in professional learning communities, group reflection and practice. * Has the confidence, resilience and optimism to maintain an emotional balance in challenging situations and the capacity to work through these situations. * Is open and responsive to constructive feedback regarding their professional practice and, if necessary, seeks appropriate support, advice and guidance. * Is aware of the Teaching Council Code of Professional Conduct and Social Media Guidelines and is familiar with its professional requirements. * Practices within the statutory framework and regulations pertaining to education, including child protection and health and safety guidelines. * Upholds professional integrity for example discretion, confidentiality, loyalty and trust, empathy etc in all aspects of their professional role as a teacher. * Is aware of the broader student context and their requirement to model best practice in all aspect of their role and relationships in the school community. Please check your email (including spam folders) for updates. If you have any technical issues when applying for this position, please contact Jobtrain at; https://jobseekersupport.jobtrain.co.uk/support/home where you can log a ticket or call 0044 161 850 2004\. FAQ's are available in the top right menu once you have registered. (Please note Jobtrain hours are from 9am to 5\.30pm, and queries may take up to 24hrs to be answered).

title: IT Solutions Architect
description: **Job Title** IT Solutions Architect **Salary** €76, 202 pa (Other arrangements may apply of you are currently a serving civil or public servant) **Tenure** 5\-year fixed term contract **Work Location** Dublin office **Closing Date** Midnight, Thursday 2nd July 2026 **Role Profile** We have an exciting opportunity in SEAI for a Solutions Architect in the IT Department which is part of the Corporate Services Directorate. The IT Department provides support across all aspects of SEAI operations, with teams focused on several areas: IT Project and Business System Programmes (PMO), Enterprise Architecture, Data Governance, IT Operations including IT Helpdesk, IT Governance, IT Change Management, and Cyber and Information Security. The successful candidate will join the current Enterprise Architect and support them in defining, maintaining, and governing the SEAIs IT systems architecture to ensure that business strategy objectives, and costeffective technology are aligned. Reporting to the Chief Information Officer (CIO), the successful candidate will work across the IT Department and third\-party vendors to develop and integrate ICT systems, reviewing existing systems to identify areas for improvement and monitoring the outcome of new and upgrades business systems. The successful candidate will join an enthusiastic and innovative team who are committed to the high\-quality delivery of SEAI's ambitious ICT Strategy and customer centric digital services. **Essential Requirements** The successful candidate must be able to demonstrate: * A degree (minimum Level 8 on the NFQ or higher) in IT, Computer Science, or other relevant discipline. * A minimum of 5 years directly relevant experience working in IT Architecture design and implementation within large complex organisations (a master's degree may account for 1\-years' experience). * Demonstrated proficiency in system architecture principles and frameworks, software development practices, cloud platforms (Azure) and service orientated architecture (SOA), data architecture and software development practices. * Experience in establishing and operating architecture frameworks and governance. * Significant experience in stakeholder management involving a diverse range of internal and external stakeholders with complex interdependencies. * Proficiency in development methodologies such as DevOps. **Desirable Requirements** * Certificate in architecture frameworks such as TOGAF. * Experience in enterprise\-level systems design. * Experience in microservices and API first architectures. * Proficiency in the Irish Language. * Experience or knowledge of delivering positive customer experiences. **Eligibility to Work** The SEAI has a legal obligation to ensure that all employees are lawfully entitled to work in Ireland. To qualify, candidates must hold a valid visa on the date of application and where applicable to support a candidate's application, candidates must submit a valid work permit/visa confirming permission to work in Ireland when requested. Failure to submit the required evidence, and failure to hold a valid permit/visa during the period of the contract, will result in the application and/or contract of employment being rendered void. Note in respect of UK citizens: Information regarding the Common Travel Area is available **here** .

title: Incident Management Engineer (Portuguese/English bilingual), Incident Detection and Response
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- Amazon has built a reputation for excellence with a mission to be the earth’s most customer\-centric company, a company that customers from all over the globe will recognize, value, and trust for both our products and our service. Amazon Web Services (AWS) is carrying on that tradition while leading the world in cloud technologies. The AWS Incident Detection and Response team is part of the ASPIRE organisation within AWS Support, and is dedicated to offering eligible AWS Enterprise Support customers proactive engagement and incident management to reduce the potential for failure and to accelerate recovery of critical workloads from disruption. We achieve these objectives by working closely with customers to develop runbooks and response plans customized to the context of each workload onboarded to the service. Onboarded workloads are monitored 24x7 by a team of Incident Management Engineers (IMEs) to detect and engage customers on a call bridge within 5 minutes of a critical alarm. ABOUT YOU Incident Management Engineers have a broad skill set with demonstrated career progression and a proven track record of delivering results. The successful candidate will possess strong analytical acumen, solid technology experience, superb business judgment, strategic account ownership and a propensity to dive deep to solve complex problems. You will also have a passion for creating/providing a world class experience for our customers. The candidate must understand the competitive and industry landscape and must have the leadership presence and communication skills to effectively work with customers at all levels of their organization. You must be a self\-starter and able to execute at both a tactical and strategic level – with a strong attention to detail. This is a global role that requires excellent written and verbal communication skills and a passion and desire for leading the resolution of critical incidents. Your decisions are not only fundamental to helping protect our most critical customers but will help maintain the health of AWS customers worldwide. Finally, you are passionate about technology with a desire to learn more and do more with AWS. ABOUT THE ROLE AWS Support is looking for a leader with a strong background in Incident Management and customer ownership to be there during the moments that matter for our most critical customers. We are looking for an Incident Management Engineer to join our team to provide incident response and account ownership. In this position, you will play a pivotal role in providing communication, emergency response, technical resolver engagement and incident management for our customers. Please note as a follow\-the\-sun organisation, IMEs work the core hours of 7am\-3pm UTC. Successful applicants will be required to work some weekends (Sunday to Thursday, or Tuesday to Saturday), and public holidays. Key job responsibilities * Every day will bring new and exciting challenges that include elements of: * Drive the resolution of large scale customer impacting incidents as part of a team rotation * Drive critical, complex customer escalations in situations that are sometimes technically challenging in collaboration with Engineering Teams. * Provide critical incident response/management (including leading calls with internal/external participants) for customer’s critical workloads * Contribute to Problem Records for customers * Conduct continuous real\-time proactive monitoring of customer metrics * Prioritize, manage, and own emerging and developing customer issues from start to finish * Monitor and manage communications during high impact events via relevant channels * Collaborate with key stakeholders across AWS to improve the customer experience and develop mechanisms that support operational excellence * Lead projects and teams to drive operational improvements * Create and review documentation; design/influence new standard operating procedures * Identify and troubleshoot recurring platform issues and own projects to drive improvements * Mentor peers in your areas of technical and operational strength * Perform other duties as required by the organization About the team Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud. **BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent, or experience in technical support * Experience in network and operating system support * Experience with virtualization, orchestration and cloud computing (eg. Hypervisors, VMware, Xen) * Experience with database administration * Experience in software development with object oriented language * Experience with continuous integration and continuous delivery * Experience in information security and compliance **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * AWS certification, or Associate's degree * Knowledge of Lean methodologies * Experience dealing well with ambiguity, prioritizing needs, and delivering measurable results in an agile environment * Experience that includes strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and effective communication abilities * Experience working in a collaborative team environment to deliver high\-quality design solutions * Knowledge of distributed systems design and implementation or equivalent * Experience performing complex business case analysis to justify technical decisions and presenting the justification to management in a high\-level review Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: Information Technology Help Desk Support
description: **IT Helpdesk Technician** An I **nternational Professional Services Organisation** with offices in Dublin 2 are seeking to hire an experienced **IT Helpdesk Technician** to support their **800\+ users** with IT Support and Infrastructure maintenance. *This is a permanent role with excellent benefits and progression opportunities.* * Hybrid role; The successful candidate will be required to work on site initially for training and then move to a hybrid working model of 3 days on site per week. * Excellent benefits package which includes: 23 days Annual Leave, Health Insurance and Pension, Income Protection Insurance and Life Assurance, onsite gym etc. **Responsibilities:** * Deliver first\-level technical support to end users through phone, email, or in\-person interactions. * Accurately and efficiently record all support requests and incidents in the ticketing system. * Troubleshoot and resolve hardware, software, and network issues by diagnosing problems and researching solutions. * Escalate complex or unresolved issues to senior IT Service Desk Analysts or other IT teams within the IT Department as needed. * Ensure all service desk tickets are properly prioritised, categorised, and resolved in accordance with established service level agreements (SLAs). * Follow all established procedures and processes, including thorough documentation and follow\-up, to maintain consistent and high\-quality service delivery. * Assist with user account management, including account creation, administration, password resets, and access control. * Provide guidance and basic training to end users on software applications and IT services. * Support the IT AV technician with in\-house and external client\-facing hybrid meetings, including IT AV assistance for Microsoft Teams and Zoom meetings. **Experience \& Background:** * Minimum 2\-3 years IT Service Desk / Helpdesk experience in a fast paced environment. * Previous Professional Services experience is highly desired. * You have strong technical knowledge of operating systems, hardware and software. * You have experience in IT Applications including Microsoft Office 365, 2016 and Exchange Server. * Experience with Microsoft Azure environment is highly advantageous. If you meet the requirements, please send your CV to Sarah Friel via the enclosed link.

title: Information Technology Support Analyst
description: **IT Service Desk Analyst** **Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid 3 days in the office)** **Salary on request** A leading professional services organisation is seeking an IT Service Desk Analyst to join its growing IT team. This is an excellent opportunity for an IT support professional looking to develop their career within a fast\-paced, technology\-driven environment that supports a large user base across multiple business functions. The organisation offers a collaborative culture, strong learning and development opportunities, and exposure to modern Microsoft technologies and enterprise IT environments. As an IT Service Desk Analyst, you will act as the first point of contact for end users, delivering exceptional technical support and customer service across a range of hardware, software, and collaboration technologies. You will be responsible for resolving incidents, managing service requests, supporting Microsoft technologies, and ensuring a high standard of service delivery while adhering to agreed SLAs. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide technical support to end users via phone, email, remote support, and in person. * Diagnose and resolve hardware, software, and operating system issues. * Support Windows\-based desktop and laptop environments. * Configure, deploy, image, and maintain end\-user devices. * Manage user accounts, permissions, password resets, and access requests. * Support Mobile Device Management (MDM) platforms and mobile devices. * Own incidents and service requests from logging through to resolution. * Ensure tickets are prioritised appropriately and resolved within SLA targets. * Provide support for Microsoft Teams, Zoom, and hybrid meeting room technology. * Create and maintain technical documentation and knowledge base articles. * Collaborate with infrastructure, networking, and end\-user computing teams. * Contribute to continuous service improvement initiatives. * Participate in an on\-call support rota when required. **Requirements:** You will have experience working in a busy IT support environment and possess strong troubleshooting and customer service skills. **Required Experience** * Min 2 years' experience in an IT Service Desk, IT Support, or Desktop Support role. * Experience supporting users within a corporate or professional services environment. Strong knowledge of: * Windows Operating Systems * Microsoft 365 * Exchange Online * Active Directory * Experience supporting desktop, laptop, and peripheral hardware. * Experience with device imaging, deployment, and configuration. * Familiarity with IT ticketing systems and ITIL principles. * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills. * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. **Desirable Skills** * Microsoft Intune * Mobile Device Management (MDM) * Azure * Microsoft Teams administration * Hybrid Microsoft environments **What's on Offer** * Hybrid working model * Exposure to modern Microsoft technologies * Strong learning and development opportunities * Collaborative and supportive team environment * Opportunity to work within a highly regarded professional services organisation * Competitive salary and benefits package If you are an IT Support professional looking to join a high\-performing IT team where you can continue to develop your technical and customer\-facing skills, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Intern - Corporate Investment and Business Advisory (Summer Internship)
description: **Application Deadline:** 30 July 2026 **Department:** Corporate Investment and Business Advisory **Location:** Dublin **Description** BDO is the Irish member firm of BDO International, a global network spanning over 1,000 offices and recognised as the world’s fifth largest accountancy and advisory organisation. BDO provides a comprehensive range of services across Audit, Tax and Advisory, supporting clients from ambitious SMEs to large international businesses. We are currently seeking a motivated and detail\-oriented **Summer Intern** to join our **Corporate Investment \& Business Advisory (CIBA)** team. title: Investment Associate
description: Our client is one of the world’s largest and most successful investment firms with a 40\+ year track record of investment excellence and they are currently looking to hire and Investment Associate / Senior Associate to join their team. This position will sit on a team that is responsible for the analysis of their Asset\-Based Finance (ABF) portfolio. **Responsibilities will include:** * Monitoring of Asset Based Finance trading performance through detailed financial statements analysis \& dialogue with the company * Asset Based Finance, Asset\-Backed Securitisation, Asset\-Backed Securities (ABS), Asset\-Based Lending * Investment analysis * Financial modelling and sensitivity analysis * Preparation of quarterly reviews and presentation to Investment Committees **The key attributes required from successful candidates are:** * 4\-7 years relevant experience (i.e. investment banking, infrastructure, real estate, aircraft leasing, corporate finance, private equity, transaction services or restructuring) * Excellent academic background * ACA or CFA qualified not essential but prefered * Strong commercial awareness and a deep understanding of financial statements / financial modelling This role pays a market leading compensation package. If you are interested in discussing this role further, please feel free to contact Dylan Philpott at d.philpott@masonalexander.ie or \+353 1 522 2943\.

title: Junior Associate - Investment Servicing
description: Summary: The Junior Associate will assist the Middle Office team in the development of large\-scale projects for high profile platforms, from Management Companies to fund clients and work in a supportive role within the Trade Execution and Investment Servicing teams. * Adhere to and follow the middle office checklist daily, including but not limited to Corporate Actions, Data Checks, Subscriptions and Redemption activity, Unfilled Order and Pending Instructions, Failed Trades, New Order Instructions and Reconciliations. * Assist the Manager with the design and implementation of internal and operating controls (especially in relation to OMS and PMS systems and all interactions with the market. * As you grown, learn to evaluate the risk involved in each situation, prioritize risk levels and escalate as appropriate. * Assist the team in ensuring all securities are settled on time and failed trades are investigated. * Currency Hedging including the daily production of the hedge fund workbook for review by the supervisor and instruction of hedge adjustments required which must be approved by supervisor. * Daily reconciliation between Middle Office records and depositary/custodian holdings. * Liaise daily with the trade execution team to ensure the trade execution, settlement and reconciliation process is adhered to. * Follow all pre\-trade compliance procedures and checks, escalate breaches. * Assist in the on\-boarding of new clients, broker and depositary/custodians. * As instructed by your client and approved by the supervisor, structure and process all payments across multiple platforms and service providers. * Ensure all corporate actions (elective and mandatory) are processed and accounted for. * Actively participate in fund governance meetings and attend technical training. * Assists colleagues when capacity permits and notifies supervisor with availability. * Foster positive team environment and ensure open communication. * Actively participate in team projects as instructed by the supervisor. * Develop and maintain detailed knowledge of Waystone operations and the regulatory implications thereof. * Adhere to Waystone change management procedures in order to ensure efficient implementations and minimal client impact. * Assist the team in the development, implementation and monitoring of policies and procedural manuals that contribute to the efficiency of the department while also ensuring timely and accurate delivery of service and regulatory compliance. * Ensure all regulatory and compliance requirements are met for an assigned portfolio of clients. * Communicate with clients to ensure satisfaction with the service provided and to resolve any potential issues. * When instructed, independently liaise with fund service providers and fund legal counsels. To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. **Education**: * A third level degree in Business, finance and/or related area. **Experience**: * Ideally, candidates will have some prior hedge fund documentation and client service exposure or have a paralegal or corporate administrator background. * Have a strong interest in the Financial Services Sector. * Outstanding time management, organizational skills, oral/written communication skills, critical thinking and attention to detail is required. * Must be able to work in a fast\-paced environment including reporting and providing support to various members of the team. * Strong MS Office and Analytic Skills; mid\-level information management software experience required.

title: Junior Building Services Coordinator
description: **Job Description:** **Overview** We are currently seeking a Junior Building Services Coordinator to join a leading construction contractor in Dublin. This is an excellent opportunity for a graduate or candidate with 1\-3 years of experience looking to develop their career in Building Services, MEP Coordination, and Project Delivery. Working alongside experienced Project Managers, Engineers, and Building Services Coordinators, you will support the coordination and delivery of mechanical, electrical, and plumbing (MEP) services across a range of high\-profile construction projects. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in the coordination of mechanical, electrical, and plumbing (MEP) services on construction projects. * Review drawings, specifications, and technical submittals to ensure compliance with project requirements. * Support design coordination meetings with consultants, subcontractors, and project teams. * Monitor and track the progress of building services installations on site. * Assist with the management of RFIs, technical queries, and design documentation. * Coordinate BIM models and support clash detection processes where required. * Ensure services installations are delivered in line with programme, quality, and safety requirements. * Support commissioning, testing, and handover activities. * Maintain project records, reports, and coordination schedules. **Requirements** * Degree or Diploma in Building Services Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Construction Management, or a related discipline. * 1\-3 years experience in a Building Services, MEP, Mechanical, Electrical, or Construction\-related role. * Strong understanding of mechanical and electrical building services systems. * Excellent communication and organisational skills. * Ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary project team. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite. * Experience with AutoCAD, Revit, or BIM software is advantageous. * Full driving licence preferred. **What's on Offer** * Competitive salary and benefits package. * Structured training and mentorship from experienced professionals. * Clear career progression opportunities. * Exposure to large\-scale commercial, residential, pharmaceutical, and data centre projects. * Ongoing professional development and support towards further qualifications. If you are looking to advance your career in Building Services and gain experience on some of Ireland's most exciting construction projects, we would love to hear from you.

title: Junior Consultant, Data Center Technician / Network Engineer CCNA
description: **The Opportunity** With offices in Pennsylvania, United States, Dublin, Ireland and Singapore, Island Networks is an innovative IT Services company that provides cloud and converged IT infrastructure solutions to large enterprises globally. Since its inception in 2012 Island Networks has built a world class team of professionals along with a customer base of blue\-chip global organizations and in recent years the company has experienced rapid growth, particularly in the R\&D and high\-tech vertical industry sectors. The culture in Island Networks is one where attitude and ability is favored over experience. **Job Description** As a Junior Consultant, you will be an integral member of the Professional Services Consulting team, supporting the delivery of technology solutions to our customers. This role focuses on developing foundational skills in cloud and data center technologies, with exposure to enterprise storage and networking environments. You will work under the guidance of senior team members to assist in the design, implementation, and support of multi\-vendor solutions in both pre\- and post\-sales engagements. **Key Responsibilities** Design and Implementation Support * Assist in the design of storage and network architecture based on business requirements and established best practices. * Support the implementation of storage solutions such as SAN (Storage Area Network) and NAS (Network Attached Storage), including NetApp systems. * Help configure and deploy network devices including routers, switches, firewalls, and load balancers under supervision. Storage Operations * Assist with storage provisioning, basic capacity planning, and performance monitoring. * Support backup and disaster recovery processes to ensure data protection. * Monitor storage environments and escalate issues as needed. Network Operations * Support the configuration and maintenance of LAN, WAN, and WLAN environments. * Assist in monitoring network performance and troubleshooting connectivity issues. * Help implement basic network security measures such as firewalls and VPN configurations. Data Center Operations (Racking, Cabling, and Facilities Coordination) * Assist with rack\-and\-stack activities (mounting servers, storage, and network gear) following vendor and internal standards. * Perform structured cabling tasks including patching, labeling, and cable management for copper and fiber connections. * Maintain accurate rack elevations, port mappings, and asset/inventory records; update diagrams and build documentation as changes are made. * Support day\-to\-day data center operations including receiving/returning equipment (RMA), coordinating access, following change windows, and updating tickets and work logs. Collaboration and Support * Work closely with internal teams to support integration of storage and network infrastructure. * Provide first\-level technical support and assist in troubleshooting storage and network\-related issues. * Maintain and update documentation for configurations, procedures, and issue resolution. Security and Compliance * Follow established security policies and procedures to help protect infrastructure. * Assist in maintaining compliance with relevant regulatory and industry standards. **Required Skills and Qualifications** * 1–3 years of experience in IT infrastructure, storage, or networking (internships or academic experience considered). * Basic understanding of SAN/NAS storage concepts and enterprise environments. * Familiarity with storage technologies (NAS, SAN, iSCSI, Fibre Channel, NFS). * Foundational knowledge of networking concepts including TCP/IP, VLANs, and routing basics. * Exposure to tools and platforms such as VMware vSphere, Cisco UCS, or NetApp (preferred but not required). * Basic understanding of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) is a plus. * Strong problem\-solving skills with a willingness to learn and grow. * Good communication and collaboration skills. * Must be able to lift a minimum of 22kg (50lbs) * An active drivers license and insured vehicle is needed for this position. **Preferred Certifications** * Entry\-level certifications such as Cisco CCNA or NetApp Certified Technology Associate (NCTA) are a plus. **Additional Requirements** * Willingness to work occasional off\-hours for maintenance or support activities. * Self\-motivated with a desire to learn and develop technical expertise. * Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality. * Interest in staying current with emerging technologies in storage and networking. * Ability to travel internationally to client sites in EU / Related Regions for projects as needed *Applicants must be authorized to work in the EU now and in the future, Island Networks cannot provide sponsorship to visas. Island Networks is an Equal Opportunity Employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type and affords equal employment opportunities to employees and applicants without regard to race, traits associated with race, creed, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, age, national origin, disability status, genetic information, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law.*

title: Junior Sales Executive
description: **About Coatek** At Coatek, we specialise in transforming spaces into visually appealing, comfortable, and brand\-aligned environments. We design, produce, and install innovative solutions including wall and glass graphics, specialised window films, signage, and architectural finishes. Our mission is simple but powerful: *Design, produce, and install cost\-effective and sustainable solutions that not only make spaces more comfortable but also resonate with the brand's identity.* **The Opportunity** We are seeking an enthusiastic and motivated Junior Sales Executive to help drive new business opportunities in both commercial and residential markets. This is an excellent opportunity for someone who enjoys building relationships, generating leads, and helping customers find the right solutions for their homes and businesses. Working closely with the Managing Director and sales team, you will be responsible for identifying opportunities, nurturing leads, and supporting the sales process from initial enquiry through to quotation and job completion. **Key Responsibilities** * Generate and qualify new sales leads through phone, email, website enquiries, and social media channels. * Follow up on inbound enquiries and convert them into sales opportunities. * Build and maintain relationships with residential customers and prospective clients. * Schedule appointments and site surveys for one to two on\-the\-road days per week. * Prepare and send quotations * Maintain accurate records of customer interactions and opportunities within the CRM system. * Conduct follow\-up calls and emails to progress quotations and enquiries. * Process site signage orders and related documentation. * Support marketing campaigns and promotional activities aimed at generating new business. * Identify upselling and cross\-selling opportunities. * Work towards agreed sales targets and KPIs. **About You** To succeed in this role, you will have: * Previous experience in sales, customer service, or lead generation. * Strong communication and relationship\-building skills. * Confidence speaking with customers by phone and email. * A positive, self\-motivated attitude with a willingness to learn. * Good organisational and time\-management skills. * Proficiency with Microsoft Office and CRM systems. * Ability to work independently while contributing to a team environment. * A customer\-focused approach and passion for delivering excellent service. **Preferred Experience** * 1–3 years' experience in sales or lead generation * Experience using CRM systems. **What We Offer** * Opportunity to develop a career in sales. * Supportive and collaborative team environment. * Training and development opportunities. Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * On\-site parking Experience: * Sales: 2 years (preferred) * CRM software: 2 years (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Junior Web Developer
description: We are looking for a Junior Web Developer to join our team \- this is an excellent opportunity to push your web development skills to the next level as part of an award winning digital marketing agency. Wolfgang Digital is one of Ireland’s and Europe’s most decorated performance digital marketing agencies, winning Best Large Integrated Agency 2 years in a row at the Global Search Awards. Our mission is to continually deliver exceptional digital marketing that drives extraordinary business growth for our 100\+ clients. Our culture is one of ongoing learning and development. We invest a lot of time each month in upskilling \& development to ensure our team’s technical \& communication skills are constantly improving. **NB** **This is a hybrid role. We work in our office in Dublin city centre on Wednesdays, Thursdays \& Fridays. The rest of the week you decide where you work, in the office or remotely.** **The Role:** * Junior Web Developer * Manage relationships and communication with both colleagues and clients * Working closely with senior colleagues to develop business intelligence applications such as time management and pipeline management * Liaise with our SEO, Social, Content, Creative, Paid Search, CRM \& Marketing Automation and internal teams as and when needed for relevant dev work. **The Candidate:** * A bachelor's degree in information technology, computer science, or a similar field.
