understanding of how web applications work including security, session management, and best development practices * Adequate knowledge of relational database systems and web application development * Ability to work and thrive in a fast\-paced environment, learn rapidly and master diverse web technologies and techniques. * Experience with Next.js and React * Proficiency in TypeScript * Experience with Supabase (or similar PostgreSQL\-based backend\-as\-a\-service platforms) * Experience with API's * Experience with Git workflows and version control **Desirables:** * SEO experience (under\-the\-hood aspects of a website that can affect crawling, UX, website structure, website speed, robots.txt, htaccess) * Knowledge of Google Tag Manager, Google Analytics, Shopify, Unbounce, and Wordpress CMS * Experience working with HTML, CSS and Javascript * Solid understanding of web design and development principles. * Experience with AWS (Amazon Web Services) or similar (Google Cloud, etc.) * Integrating data from various back\-end services and databases * Accomplished user of Google Analytics * Interest in using AI tools to enhance development workflow * Familiarity with AI coding assistants **Wolfgang Digital is:** * We are the First Irish Company to Transition to Employee Owned * After 1 year with us, you’ll be eligible for Employee Ownership Profit share * Certified Great Place to Work (Ranked \#7 Best Workplaces In Ireland™ 2026 \- Mid\-Size) * Paying a market\-rate salary with the opportunity for performance bonuses * Bike to Work Scheme and Tax Saver Travel Schemes available * Wellbeing perks including massages, counselling, and medical checks * An inclusive, equitable and diverse workplace * Hybrid work model (we are in the office three days a week) * Flexi\-Time * Training and development opportunities * Continuous professional development through the Wolfgang Academy * Team social events every quarter * Annual tree planting events with Wolfgang Reforest * Big on winning awards \- you'll be challenged to become World\-Leading (Our Awards)

title: Law Enforcement Response Specialist, EMEA - Trust & Safety
description: **Responsibilities** About the team Our Trust \& Safety team's commitment is to keep our online community safe. We have invested heavily in human and machine\-based moderation to remove harmful content quickly and often before it reaches our general community. About the role The Law Enforcement Response Team (LERT) is part of the Trust \& Safety team. As a LERT Specialist, you will be responsible for analysing and responding to time\-sensitive user data requests, preservation requests and inquiries from law enforcement in the EEA/UK. You will work in a global and cross\-functional team to ensure user safety on the platform and mitigate legal risk. You take great pride in knowing that every day you are working hard to promote trust in our platform and ensuring the highest levels of user safety. Note: This position will include reviewing content that may be offensive or sensitive to some people and will include after hours work and weekend duty. What will I be doing? \- Reviewing and responding to an increasing number of user data and preservation requests from law enforcement agencies while protecting user privacy. \- Ensuring that all law enforcement requests are analysed and processed in compliance with applicable laws (e.g., Digital Services Act, e\-Evidence Regulation), internal Standard Operating Procedures, and our law enforcement guidelines, with a strong focus on evolving European legal frameworks. \- Proactively monitoring and analysing the implementation and impact of the upcoming e\-Evidence Regulation, and contribute to the adaptation of internal workflows and procedures to ensure full compliance. \- Collaborating with cross‑functional internal teams such as legal and external partners to deal with issues concerning user safety, data privacy and data disclosure. \- Ensuring operational excellence while upholding high quality, speed, and accuracy in your work. \- Gathering and analysing data on law enforcement requests to improve internal processes and consistently communicating with both internal and external stakeholders. \- Drafting internal documentation, workflows and procedures concerning user safety, data privacy and data disclosure. \- Keeping accurate internal records and clearly articulating operational decisions. \- Working on sensitive issues that include child exploitation, graphic violence, self\-injury and other content which may be considered offensive or disturbing. **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications: \- Bachelor's degree and/or equivalent experience and a minimum of 3 years experience in a Law Enforcement Response specialist role or similar fields. \- Experience in the retrieval and formatting of data using various internal tools and platforms which continue to evolve and develop. \- Fluency in English and advanced English writing skills. \- Proven ability to analyse and accurately process data with exceptional attention to detail in a fast\-moving environment; requires strong analytical, organisational, and time\-management skills to work independently and prioritise effectively under tight deadlines. \- Understanding of the legal mechanisms for law enforcement data access in the EEA/UK region, including Mutual Legal Assistance Treaties (MLAT), European Production and Preservation Orders, and national judicial orders. \- Experience in navigating and complying with complex, strict regulatory environments, preferably with direct experience related to data privacy and disclosure rules in the technology sector. \- Collaborative mindset with the ability to adapt and remain resilient while tackling challenging issues in a dynamic, multi\-cultural, and fast paced work environment Preferred Qualifications: \- Bachelor's degree in law, criminal justice or similar. \- Experience in developing or refining operational policies and procedures in response to new legal or regulatory requirements. Recruitment Process: \- Along with your resume, please submit a cover letter detailing your suitability for the role. **Trust \& Safety** Content that this role interacts with includes images, video, and text related to every\-day life, but it can also include (but is not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child safety; depictions of harm to self and others, and harm to animals. Hence, it is possible that this role will be exposed to harmful content on a daily basis. ​ ​ TikTok recognises that keeping our platform safe for the TikTok communities is no ordinary job which can be both rewarding and psychologically demanding and emotionally taxing for some. This is why we are sharing the potential hazards, risks and implications in this unique line of work from the start, so our candidates are well informed before joining. ​ ​ We are committed to the wellbeing of all our employees and promise to provide comprehensive and evidence\-based programs, to promote and support physical and mental wellbeing throughout each employee's journey with us. We believe that wellbeing is a relationship and that everyone has a part to play, so we work in collaboration and consultation with our employees and across our functions in order to ensure a truly person\-centred, innovative and integrated approach.​ **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​ **Why Join Us** Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day.​ We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​ ​ Diversity \& Inclusion​ TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​

The role supports a broad range of activities within the CLO Operations team, including, but not limited to, Asset Maintenance, Daily Trade Activity, MIS, Deal Closings, Trustee Reporting, and Portfolio Manager support. The successful candidate will also gain exposure to colleagues in our San Francisco, Gurugram, London, and New York offices. Key Responsibilities * Trustee Reporting * Payment Date Calculations * New Deal and Transaction Closings * Portfolio Manager Support * Management of relationships with third\-party Custodians and Administrators (US Bank, BNY Mellon, FIS Virtus) * Reviewing daily exception reporting * Ad hoc duties assisting other team members as required **Skills And Experience Required** * Demonstrated interest in the firm and the market, with a desire to learn about the firm and a broad range of investment products and activities * Ability to work on own initiative and demonstrate a proactive approach * Ability to work in a fast\-paced, challenging environment with changing priorities * Strong communication skills, both written and verbal * Strong academic background * Excellent interpersonal and teamwork skills, with the ability to work effectively with individuals at all levels of the organisation * Professionalism * Strong MS Excel skills with an analytical mindset * Prior CLO knowledge preferred * Prior experience with Advent Geneva and Allvue BMS Everest, or a similar product stack * A strong focus on improving processes and executing at scale KKR is an equal opportunity employer. Individuals seeking employment are considered without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, or any other category protected by applicable law. KKR will provide reasonable accommodations as required by applicable federal, state, and/or local laws. Individuals seeking an accommodation for the application or interview process should email Benefits@kkr.com. Emails sent for unrelated issues, such as following up on an application, will not receive a response. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access https://www.kkr.com/careers because of your disability. You can request reasonable accommodations by sending an email to Benefits@kkr.com. Only emails left for this purpose will be returned. **Massachusetts Applicants:** It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. This notice applies only to applicants and employees who work or will work in Massachusetts, in accordance with applicable state law.

title: Mobile Generator Fitter
description: **Role:** Mobile Fitter – Generator **Location:** Portlaoise **Company:** Laois Hire – part of The Briggs Equipment Group **Salary:** Competitive \+ Company Vehicle **Contract:** Permanent **About Us:** Briggs Equipment Group is a leading provider of asset management and maintenance solutions across Ireland and the UK. Briggs Equipment Ireland is part of a €100 million turnover group, businesses in our group include Briggs Equipment Ireland, Laois Hire Services, Aerial Platform Hire, Future Events Hire and Galway Plant and Tool Hire. Laois Hire is a leading plant and tool hire company in Ireland that provide a comprehensive range of services from tool and plant machinery to highly specialised equipment. We have a **new opportunity** for an enthusiastic and driven **Mobile Generator Fitter** to join our team in **Portlaoise**. **In this role you will be:** * Performing servicing and repairs on rental equipment both on\-site and at the depot. * Preparing machinery and equipment for hire, ensuring operational readiness. * Diagnosing and repairing mechanical and electrical faults on generators and pumps. * Conducting testing on generators to verify performance and reliability. * Support depot operations by providing technical expertise and engineering assistance. **What will help you to excel in this role:** * Relevant technical qualification * Previous experience working on generators and pumps. * Good understanding of hydraulic, mechanical and electrical principals * The ability to work independently and under pressure. * Good communication skills **What you can expect from us:** * Competitive salary * Contributory pension scheme with employer contributions up to 6% * HSF – Healthcare * Future development and career opportunities * Profitshare bonus based on business performance **What’s next** If you are interested in joining The Briggs Group, then please click on the apply now button and a member of the team will be in touch. Not sure if this is the right role for you? Please feel free to visit the careers page on our website and send us your CV speculatively for a member of the team to review. If you require any reasonable adjustments to assist you in the interview process, then please contact a member of the Recruitment Team, and inform them prior to attending so we can ensure these are met.

title: Network Engineer
description: Virtu is a leading financial firm that leverages cutting edge technology to deliver liquidity to the global markets and innovative, transparent trading solutions to our clients. As a market maker, Virtu provides deep liquidity that helps to create more efficient markets around the world. Our market structure expertise, broad diversification, and execution technology enables us to provide competitive bids and offers in over 19,000 securities, at over 235 venues, in 36 countries worldwide. **THE ROLE** Virtu is looking for an enthusiastic and proactive Network Engineer to join our global network engineering team. In this role, you'll help enhance our world leading network platform to grow alongside our ever expanding business. You will be working at the forefront of the world's financial markets, building connectivity across the globe, and integrating some of the most complex networks in the industry. Automation should be second nature for our ideal candidate. Additional duties include: * Design, implement and operate Virtu's network. Whether it is investigating a new, innovative products, refreshing one of our low latency trading systems or troubleshooting with a 3rd party, you'll thrive on being impactful within a fast\-moving, varied environment. * Transforming our way of working with automation and scale. We know the network industry is changing, and this is your chance to make that change, collaborating with software engineers with proven track records of solving complex problems through automation and software. **THE CANDIDATE** * Required skills: * + 5\+ years of experience in network engineering + Real\-world network automation experience - Configuration Management – one of Ansible/Salt/Puppet/Chef - Python (or other programming languages) + Expert in routing and switching – BGP/OSPF/Multicast + Deep understanding of TCP/IP and the OSI model + Good prioritization skills and the ability to effectively switch tasks on varying projects and initiatives + Excellent communication and writing skills + Humility + A love of technology in general * Highly desired skills: * + Comfortable working with various Linux utilities and different OS + Network security, including firewalls Fortinet/Palo Alto/CheckPoint + SQL + A bachelor's degree, preferably in IT, Computer Science or other related discipline **PERKS OF THE ROLE:** * Projects with quick life cycles, from concept to production in three months * The opportunity to make an impact in a highly visible role within the firm * A great place for upward and geographic mobility * Direct exposure to the decision makers and senior leaders on the business side * A company that's investing a sizable amount in its technology department * Teams that are passionate about continually learning, improving and raising the bar * A community that values hard work as well as work\-life balance *Virtu Financial is an equal opportunity employer, committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, welcoming you for who you are and does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or other legally protected status.*

title: New Business & Seller Growth Executive - TikTok Shop, French Speaker (Third-party Associate)
description: **Responsibilities** TikTok Shop is an innovative marketplace fully integrated into TikTok. It brings together online sellers, buyers and creators \- all within TikTok to build a one\-stop, seamless shopping experience and bringing more commerce solutions that further enable our community to discover and engage with what they love. We are seeking a driven and proactive Sales Development Representative (SDR) to be a critical engine for our expansion. This is a high\-impact, fixed\-term contract where you will identify, reach out, and onboard the next wave of high\-potential sellers. And later work with these sellers to grow their GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) on our platform in close collaboration with our cross\-functional teams. The right candidate would have Sales Development acumen and experience. If you thrive on autonomy, possess exceptional communication and presentation skills, and are ready to take full ownership of high\-stakes goals in an evolving, high\-velocity environment, we want to hear from you. \- Proactively conduct outbound prospecting via multi\-channel (email, phone, social) outbound campaigns to source and qualify high\-potential seller\-partners. \- Lead initial pitches to understand seller needs, translating the unique value of TikTok Shop into compelling, tailored propositions for them. \- Assess seller fit to confirm potential partners, ensuring strong platform fit, identifying the seller growth strategies, and clear pathways to GMV growth. \- Support end\-to\-end seller onboarding, registration, account setup, and activation of new sellers. \- Collaborate with internal teams to ensure continued support for onboarded sellers. Including merchant navigation through their growth on the platform. \- Meet and exceed monthly/quarterly targets related to seller acquisition and seller GMV growth within a 3\-6 months period. \- Understand the TikTok Shop platform, the competition, and our unique positioning in the industry. **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications: \- Proven B2B Sales Acumen: Of successful B2B sales development (SDR/BDR) or full\-cycle sales experience. \- Communication Excellence: Exceptional command of English \& French, both written and verbal, for external negotiations and presentations. \- Self\-Driven \& Organised: Demonstrated strong time management, organisational skills, and the ability to operate autonomously in a remote\-first setting. \- Contract Commitment: Availability for a 6 month fixed\-term contract, with opportunity for extension based on impact and results. Preferred Qualifications: \- eCommerce experience: previous direct experience within a high\-growth eCommerce, online marketplace, or platform business is a strong advantage. \- Startup passion: Experience operating effectively within a hyper\-scaling, fluid, or startup\-like environment. Important Note: Please be advised that this job posting is on behalf of a third\-party agency. This is a 6 month temporary assignment managed by a third\-party agency, who will be your employer. While you may be assigned to work at TikTok, you will not be a TikTok employee. All contractual terms, including payroll and benefits, will be handled by a third\-party agency. By applying, you agree that the information provided in your application may be processed and retained by TikTok for recruitment purposes and shared with a third\-party agency in accordance with TikTok's Applicant Privacy Notice https://lifeattiktok.com/legal/privacy **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​ **Why Join Us** Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day.​ We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​ ​ Diversity \& Inclusion​ TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​

title: OSS SCADA Engineer
description: **Vacancy Number:** 28613 **Category:** Engineering \- Other **Job Type:** Full\-time Poisition Description The DMSO (Distribution Markets and System Operation) Operations Systems Support (OSS) team is seeking to fill the role of Senior SCADA Engineer. The role of the OSS team is to support and maintain ESB Networks critical Operational Technology (OT) systems which include SCADA, Network Management System (NMS) and Geographic Information System (GIS). SCADA is the real time operations system used in the National Distribution Control Centre (NDCC) to control and monitor the distribution network. It enables the NDCC to operate the network under maximum safety, isolating faults and restoring supply in the event of an outage. The Senior SCADA Engineer will be responsible for supporting and maintaining the SCADA system and supporting the delivery of new projects required to meet the strategic objectives of the DMSO. Support and maintenance of the SCADA system, includes data engineering, development of HMI displays, system and database administration, support, maintenance, commissioning and project work/system upgrades. The SCADA Engineer will also be responsible for the developing, maintaining and testing Remote Terminal Unit (RTU) configurations. Key Responsibilities Support and maintain 24x7 operation of the SCADA systems for the ESB Networks and the National Distribution Control Centre. Data engineering for SCADA integration works, development of HMI displays, reports and signal lists. Support commissioning for new and existing substations and distribution automation devices. Support commissioning for new and existing substations and distribution automation devices. Development of procedures, processes and work instructions for supporting and maintaining the SCADA system. Contribute to the development and implementation of SCADA solutions to meet ESB Networks' overall strategy for the safe management and operation of the distribution system or similar. Provide support to ensure the smooth roll out and reliable operation of the SCADA, and Distribution Automation Systems nationally. Support the delivery of new projects required to meet the strategic objectives of the DMSO. Stay abreast of best practice developments in network operation systems. Developing, maintain and test Remote Terminal Unit (RTU) configurations. Contribute to the development of advanced SCADA and Distribution Management Systems (DMS) applications. Provide support to the OT Cybersecurity Manager. Ensure effective use of systems by users through the provision of training programmes. Monitor Service Level Agreements with key stakeholders as required. . Working with Vendors to resolve technical issues and evaluate new systems and functionality. Stakeholder management with internal business units, third parties and system vendors. Experience and Qualifications Essential Minimum of 5\+ years relevant experience is essential. Either 3rd Level qualification or equivalent work experience is essential. Proven ability to deliver on the “key responsibilities” outlined above. A strong Knowledge of SCADA systems. A strong Knowledge of substation RTUs and Substation Control Systems (SCS) systems. Strong Knowledge of industry standard SCADA communication protocols, e.g. IEC 60870\-5\-101, 104, IEC 61850 and Inter Control Centre Protocol (ICCP). Knowledge of SCADA operations and commissioning practices. A strong work ethic with a clear focus on delivery to ESB Networks safety, time, quality and cost objectives. Knowledge of substation control wiring and equipment. The demonstrated ability to meet deadlines and manage multiple priorities. Self starter with ability to manage own work and support others within project team. The ability to technically mentor junior team members. A flexible approach to work, especially where necessary to deliver excellent customer service. Strong interpersonal skills. Excellent communication and report writing skills. Desirable Experience in Control Centre SCADA and telecommunications. Knowledge of Networking, TCP/IP and internet technology. Familiarity with Unix, Linux or similar Operating Systems. Reporting to SCADA Manager Location ESB Networks, Leopardstown Road, Dublin 18 Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration at least two days per week . All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business. Why Work with Us? Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future Career development through mentoring and training Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities Sports and Social Clubs Networking opportunities Credit Union Generous Pension Access to staff well\-being programmes Generous parental leave entitlements Strong values\-based and inclusive culture Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion **Great team environment working to our Core Values:** Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted Salary €59,000 \- €70,000 per annum Closing Date 8th of July, 2026 Interview Candidates who are shortlisted for interview will complete a Behavioural Interview based on each of the following Behaviours, which underpin how we work at ESB and are critical to delivering our strategy: Accountable and Ethical Collaborative Growing and Adaptive Proactive Respectful and Supportive More information available on Our People Promise Notes Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role. The successful candidate will be required to evidence their right to work in the Republic of Ireland / UK prior to commencement of employment. Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all sections of society and ensure that no one is discriminated against on the grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnicity or nationality, disability, age, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by law. Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment@esb.ie. About ESB Join us in our mission to achieve a net\-zero electricity system by 2040\. ESB is Ireland’s leading energy utility, with activities spanning electricity generation, transmission and distribution, energy supply, energy services and international consultancy. Operating in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain, we invest over €1 billion each year to deliver a new energy future based on reliable, affordable zero\-carbon electricity.

title: Online Fulfilment Warehouse Operative
description: **Our people make the difference.** Here at PHX Ireland, we strive to be a place for everyone where each and every one of us can be our true, authentic selves, every single day. For us, individuality sparks new ideas, shapes new approaches and brings us powerful new perspectives. It’s the heartbeat of our innovation, creativity, and success. Which is why, at PHX Ireland, we don’t just embrace our differences – we celebrate them. Across United Drug, McCabes Pharmacy, and TCP Homecare, in every location and every role, the way we work makes us who we are – and that’s something we are really proud of. Which is why we’ve summed up our ethos in our six PHX Ireland values; Customer focused, Quality driven, collaborative, ambitious, innovative, and inclusive. We currently have an exciting opportunity for an **Online Fulfilment Warehouse Operative** to join our team in our **Ballymun** Warehouse. **This is a temporary 6 month contract.** ***The care you take here, gets care to patients everywhere.*** **Why You'll Love Working With Us** 💼 We’ve got your back: employee assistance programme 🛍️ Perks you’ll actually use: Generous in store discounts 🌈 Belonging at Work: Inclusive, welcoming team culture where you’re valued for *you* . 👶 Family comes first: Life Stage Policies including maternity and paternity pay The Online Fulfilment Operative is responsible for accurately picking, packing, and dispatching customer orders for online sales. The role ensures orders are processed efficiently, safely, and to a high standard to support excellent customer service and timely delivery. **Key Responsibilities** * Pick customer orders from online warehouse locations using handheld scanners. * Pack items securely and correctly to ensure safe delivery. * Prepare orders for dispatch and ensure correct labelling and documentation. * Maintain high levels of accuracy and productivity when processing orders. * Receive and store incoming stock in the correct warehouse locations. * Assist with stock control activities, including cycle counts and stocktakes. * Ensure work areas are kept clean, organised, and safe. * Follow all health and safety procedures and company policies. * Work collaboratively with team members to meet daily fulfilment targets. * Report any stock discrepancies, damaged goods, or operational issues to supervisors. **Skills And Experience** * Previous warehouse or order fulfilment experience preferred but not essential. * Good attention to detail and accuracy when handling orders. * Ability to work efficiently in a fast\-paced environment. * Basic computer or handheld scanner skills. * Good communication and teamwork skills. * Ability to follow processes and instructions. **Physical Requirements** * Ability to stand and walk for extended periods. * Ability to lift and move boxes (up to 20kg where required). * Comfortable working in a warehouse environment. **Desirable Attributes** * Experience working with stock management systems * Forklift or manual handling certification * Flexible availability for peak trading periods. **About PHX Ireland \& PHOENIX Group** We are comprised of United Drug, Ireland’s leading pharmaceutical wholesaler and distributor; McCabes Pharmacy, Ireland’s leading community pharmacy chain; and TCP Homecare, innovators in clinical home nursing services and direct to patient delivery solutions. PHX Ireland Group employs over 1,800 people, operates 5 distribution centres and pharmacies nationwide, delivering over 85 million packs to Irish pharmacies and hospitals and serving 500,000 patients each year. PHX Ireland is part of the PHOENIX group, Europe’s leading healthcare provider. Headquartered in Mannheim, Germany, the PHOENIX group brings together more than 48,000 employees from 29 countries, includes 223 pharmaceutical distribution sites, and more than 3200 owned pharmacies and 17000 partner pharmacies. ***We strive to foster an open and inclusive environment, embracing applications from individuals of all backgrounds. Our commitment lies in ensuring an accessible interview process for all candidates. If you require additional support or accommodations to attend an interview, we kindly encourage you to reach out to*** ***jobs@mccabespharmacy.ie***

title: Operations Analyst III
description: **Work Location** : Dublin, Ireland ****Hours:**** 35 ****Line Of Business:**** Personal \& Commercial Banking ****Pay Details:**** We’re committed to providing fair and equitable compensation to all our colleagues. As a candidate, we encourage you to have an open dialogue with a member of our HR Team and ask compensation related questions, including pay details for this role. ****Job Description:**** **Position Title** : TDS Operations Analyst III, Corporate Lending Ops and Trade Finance Operations **Department** : TD Securities Global Operations, Corporate Lending and Trade Finance ****TD Securities Overview**** With more than 3,500 people in 13 offices around the world, TD Securities provides a wide range of capital market products and services to corporate, government and institutional clients who choose us for our knowledge, innovation and experience in the following key areas of finance: * Investment and Corporate Banking * Capital Markets * Interest Rate, Currency and Derivative products * Commodities ****Global Operations Overview**** With offices located around the world and with more than 650 professionals, the strength of TD Securities Global Operations lies in the diverse and dynamic group of individuals who provide ongoing support functions for TD Securities' businesses and their partners. As part of TD Securities, Global Operations professionals have the opportunity to learn end\-to\-end processes as well as create relationships with different lines of business. Challenged to learn and grow within their role, members of Global Operations are supported in their goals of innovation, excellence and commitment to customer service. ****Department Overview** :** Global Corporate Lending \& Trade Finance Operations are part of Global Operations, supporting high value corporate and institutional clients across loan servicing and trade finance activities. The team operates in a dynamic, control\-focused environment and is responsible for delivering high\-quality service, managing operational and financial risk, and supporting complex transactions across global markets. We are seeking a collaborative, client\-focused and forward\-thinking professional who can perform across both lending and trade finance functions while contributing to service excellence, risk discipline and continuous improvement. ****Job Description**** Reporting to the Manager, the successful candidate will be responsible for servicing a portfolio of high\-value corporate and institutional clients across Corporate Lending and Trade Finance Operations, ensuring operational accuracy, timely execution, strong risk management and a high standard of client service. **The Successful Candidate Will Be Required To:** * Demonstrate advanced knowledge of Corporate Lending and Trade Finance products, processes and controls, and act as a subject matter expert across both functions. * Service a portfolio of high value clients by processing and managing lending and trade finance transactions accurately, within timelines, and in accordance with policies, procedures and regulatory requirements. * Build and maintain effective relationships with clients, front office, legal, internal and external partners, and act as a key point of contact on operational and technical matters. * Review, authorize and perform secondary checks on transactions as required, including end\-of\-day controls and resolution of discrepancies to maintain a current and accurate portfolio. * Interpret and apply lending agreements, trade finance documentation, ICC rules and relevant internal policies to support sound decision\-making and the protection of the Bank’s interests. * Identify, escalate and help resolve non\-standard, complex or high\-risk transactions, including potential AML, sanctions, operational or reputational concerns. * Support complex client transactions and structures across lending and trade finance products while balancing client needs, control requirements and operational feasibility. * Research and investigate operational issues, provide analysis and reporting as needed, and recommend solutions that improve accuracy, efficiency and client experience. * Contribute to and lead process improvement, automation and documentation initiatives, including regular review and update of procedures to reflect current practices. * Provide guidance, knowledge sharing and cross\-functional support to team members, and contribute to a collaborative team environment focused on service, accountability and continuous improvement. * Prioritize and manage workload effectively, provide coverage where required, and lead by example in fostering collaboration across teams. ****Job Requirements**** * 4\+ years of experience in Corporate Lending Operations, Trade Finance Operations, or a comparable banking operations environment with strong operational and financial control requirements. * Strong experience managing relationships with high\-profile or high value clients and delivering a high standard of service in a control\-focused environment. * Ability to read, review and interpret lending agreements, trade finance instruments and related legal or operational documentation. * Working knowledge of lending platforms and/or trade finance processing, including deal set\-up, transaction processing, repayments, fees, guarantees, standby letters of credit, and related servicing activities. * Strong understanding of risk management, controls and escalation practices, with knowledge of AML, ATF, sanctions or regulatory requirements relevant to banking operations. * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, organizational and time management skills, with the ability to work independently and manage competing priorities. * Strong verbal and written communication skills and a collaborative, relationship\-oriented work style. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and relevant operational systems; experience with LoanIQ, trade finance systems, mainframe applications or reporting tools is an asset. * Bachelor's degree in finance, International Business, Business Management, Accounting or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience. * Professional designations or certifications such as CDCS, CSDG, finance or accounting credentials are considered an asset. * Flexibility to support global clients, including hybrid work expectations and schedule requirements aligned to business needs. ****Other Qualifications / Skills / Experience**** * Client\-facing experience in wholesale banking, corporate lending, trade finance or a comparable environment is an asset. * Experience reading legal and operational documentation is an asset. * Knowledge of ICC rules, trade finance practices and complex transaction structures is an asset. * Commitment to operational excellence, client service and continuous improvement. ****Who We Are**** TD Securities is one of the world's leading global financial institutions and is the fifth largest bank in North America by branches/stores. Every day, we strive to make every interaction, product, and experience remarkably human and refreshingly simple for over 27 million households and businesses in Canada, the United States and around the world. More than 95,000 TD colleagues bring their skills, talent, and creativity to foster deeper relationships, ensure disciplined execution, and build a simpler, faster banking experience. TD is deeply committed to being a leader in client experience, that is why we believe that all colleagues, no matter where they work, are client facing. Together, we are reimagining what banking can be for our clients, colleagues and communities. **Our Total Rewards Package** Our Total Rewards package reflects the investment we make in our colleagues to help them, and their families achieve their well\-being goals. Total Rewards at TD includes a base salary and several other key plans such as health and well\-being benefits, including medical coverage, paid time off, career development, and reward and recognition programs. ****Additional Information:**** We’re delighted that you’re considering building a career with TD. Through regular development conversations, training programs, and a competitive benefits plan, we’re committed to providing the support our colleagues need to thrive both at work and at home. **Colleague Development** If you’re interested in a specific career path or are looking to build certain skills, we want to help you succeed. You’ll have regular career, development, and performance conversations with your manager, as well as access to an online learning platform and a variety of mentoring programs to help you unlock future opportunities. If you’re passionate about helping clients and building deep, lasting relationships, TD offers diverse career paths where you can grow your expertise and make a meaningful impact. title: Operations Associate (FTC) - Grafton Street
description: **WHY JOIN ALO?** Mindful movement. It’s at the core of why we do what we do at ALO—it’s our calling. Because mindful movement in the studio leads to better living. It changes who yogis are off the mat, making their lives and their communities better. That’s the real meaning of studio\-to\-street: taking the consciousness from practice on the mat and putting it into practice in life. **Objective** The Operations Associate is critical in the store team and is responsible for driving to profitability and efficiency. The Operations Associate will work closely with the Operations \& Visual Manager \& Operations Lead in initiating, completing work, and providing feedback to the team to achieve optimal results supporting total store operations. **This is a 6\-month, fixed\-term contract.** **Key Job Responsibilities** * Execute inventory management processes effectively with a focus on maximizing inventory accuracy and reducing shrink * Investigate and root cause inventory accuracy issues, partnering with the General Manager to escalate process or system gaps * Support execution of key operational functions including shipment, receiving and processing, reverse transfers, sales floor recovery, intra\-day sales floor replenishment, floor set updates, and supply management * Maintain and champion strong visual standards for the sales floor * Work closely with reporting, budgets, expenses, inventory control, supplies, vendor relationships, technology, and facilities to ensure maintained and maximized * Support a safe work environment and efficient operation through strong stockroom standards and processes * Move dynamically on the retail floor to assess and fulfill the needs of the business, team and guests * Execute company\-level operations\-focused directives, projects and initiates, in partnership with the Operations \& Visual Manager \& Operations Lead. * Assists in processing shipment and ensuring product flow; including but not limited to visual merchandising placement and daily replenishment excellence * Demonstrate strong business acumen by leveraging metrics to support business\-driving strategies * Resolve client needs quickly \& effectively ensuring customer satisfaction * Identify product concerns and communicate inventory needs to support the business goals * Support other areas of the business, including Sales \& Service and Cashier, as needed **Operations Associate Qualifications** * Preferred prior work experience in a client\-centric, sales \& operational environment * Passion for customer service and delivering exceptional experiences * High energy, upbeat and enthusiastic with the ability to integrate fun and work * Self\-motivated with a desire to achieve results and excel individually, and as a team * Fosters genuine connection through empathy, compassion, integrity and building trusting relationships * Aligns with and embodies ALO's Guiding Principles * Proof of right to live and work in the UK **Operations Associate Schedule** Part\-Time associates are expected to work up to 29 hours per week; Full\-Time associates are expected to work 32\-40 hours per week with 100% of their work week in operations and floor support. All employees' availability must suit the needs of the business, which are subject to change. The General Manager determines availability needs and creates a schedule including morning, evening, and weekend shifts, which may be subject to change. All Employees are expected to be able to work from the week of and including “Black Friday” until the end of the holiday season, first week of January. **Diversity and Inclusion statement** As an Equal Opportunity Employer, ALO does not discriminate against applicants or employees because of race, colour, creed, religion, sex, national origin, veteran status, disability, age, citizenship, marital or domestic/civil partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression or because of any other status or condition protected by statutory law. We are committed to an active Inclusion, Diversity and Equal Opportunities Policy, which starts with our recruitment and selection process, and we are happy to talk flexible working. **Interview Process adjustments** If you consider yourself to require reasonable adjustments to any part of our recruitment process, we invite you to share those requirements with us when completing your application or by emailing europehr@aloyoga.com . We will make every effort to ensure your needs are met to provide a fair and transparent process of assessment. For CA residents, Job Applicant Privacy Policy HERE.

title: PMO & Automation Analyst - Technology (IT PMO)
description: **Join us** At Vodafone, we’re not just shaping the future of connectivity for our customers — we’re shaping the future for everyone who joins our team. When you work with us, you’re part of a global mission to connect people, solve complex challenges, and create a sustainable and more inclusive world. If you want to grow your career whilst finding the right balance between work and life, Vodafone offers the opportunity to help you belong and make a real impact. **Role Summary** As a **PMO \& Automation Analyst** , you will lead the transformation of project governance processes through automation and data\-driven insights. You will work closely with Project Managers, Finance, and our offshore VOIS teams to implement AI\-driven workflows, Power Automate solutions, and interactive dashboards that streamline project financial management and reporting. Your role is to reduce manual effort and improve efficiency by replacing traditional administrative tasks with smart, automated processes. This is an exciting opportunity for a proactive, tech\-savvy PMO professional with a passion for innovation, data, and continuous improvement within a dynamic enterprise environment. **What You Will Do** **Key Responsibilities:\-** **Design \& Manage Automated Workflows:** Develop and maintain automated processes (using tools like Power Automate and other AI/ML technologies) for key project governance activities, such as resource time tracking, approvals, and financial updates – ensuring these workflows run smoothly and minimize manual intervention. **Financial Governance \& Budget Tracking:** Partner with Project Managers and Finance to maintain accurate, real\-time project budgets and forecasts. Use digital tools and dashboards to track spend, forecast costs, and ensure resources are allocated efficiently. **Offshore (VOIS) Budget Coordination:** Coordinate with offshore/VOIS teams to manage and track budgets for outsourced project components. Ensure seamless integration of offshore costs into overall project financials and that all expenses are properly approved and documented. **Dynamic Reporting \& Dashboards:** Create and refine interactive dashboards and management reports (e.g., in Power BI or similar tools) that give senior stakeholders clear, real\-time visibility of project status, risks, and key metrics. Continuously improve these reporting outputs using feedback and new data techniques. **Advanced Analytics \& Insights:** Perform ad\-hoc data analysis on project and portfolio data to identify trends, anomalies, or improvement opportunities. Present insights through high\-quality visuals and presentations (e.g., slides, charts) to support decision\-making by leadership. **Governance \& Standards Ownership:** Drive consistency and best practices across the project management community by developing and promoting standard templates, automated processes, and data governance standards. Ensure that project teams adhere to these standards for efficient, aligned ways of working. **Continuous Improvement \& Innovation:** Proactively identify process inefficiencies or manual work steps in project and financial management. Conceive and implement improvements – from small process tweaks to larger automation initiatives – that increase productivity and reduce errors. **Stakeholder Engagement \& Training:** Act as a champion for new tools and processes, coaching and supporting project teams in adopting automated solutions. Provide training or guidance to colleagues on using new dashboards and workflow tools, building a culture of data\-driven decision making. **What You’ll Need** **Skills \& Experience:** **Technical Proficiency:** Hands\-on experience with (or strong aptitude to learn) automation and analytics tools such as Microsoft Power Automate, Power BI, or similar workflow/BI platforms. Ability to design simple automation scripts or use AI\-based tools to streamline processes. **Financial \& Analytical Skills:** Solid understanding of project budgeting, forecasting, and cost management principles. Comfortable working with financial data and large datasets, demonstrating strong analysis and numeracy skills with high attention to detail and accuracy. **Project \& Tool Knowledge:** Familiarity with project management processes and common tools (e.g., SharePoint, Microsoft Project or similar PM software). Proficiency with Microsoft Office suite, especially Excel for data manipulation and PowerPoint for presentation of complex information. **Problem\-Solving \& Innovation:** A proactive, curious mindset with proven ability to identify process gaps and implement creative technical solutions. Willingness to continuously learn and experiment with new technologies (AI, bots, data analytics) to improve ways of working. **Self\-Management \& Collaboration:** Ability to work independently, managing multiple priorities to deliver high\-quality outputs with minimal supervision. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills – able to work effectively with cross\-functional teams, explain technical concepts in simple terms, and influence stakeholders to embrace new processes. **Desirable** **PMO/Project Experience:** Previous experience in a Project Management Office, IT project delivery, or digital transformation support role, especially within a large or complex organisation. **Automation Credentials:** Exposure to robotic process automation (RPA), simple scripting (e.g., VBA, Python), or AI\-driven applications in a business context. Any relevant certification in Microsoft Power Platform or data analytics would be an advantage. **Global/Offshore Collaboration:** Experience working with offshore or remote teams and an understanding of cultural dynamics in a global organisation. **Industry Knowledge:** Familiarity with telecommunications or technology projects and understanding of their common challenges. **What We’re Looking For:** **A Tech\-Enthusiastic Problem Solver:** A motivated, detail\-oriented individual who gets excited about leveraging technology and data to drive efficiency and improve processes. **A Collaborative Influencer:** Someone who enjoys engaging with diverse stakeholders (from Project Managers to senior leaders and offshore colleagues) and can influence others to adopt new tools and practices through clear communication and demonstration of value. **A Change Champion:** A team player who embraces innovation and values governance, balancing consistency with continuous improvement. You are not afraid to challenge the status quo and can navigate change positively, acting as a champion for new ways of working. **A Growth Mindset:** An individual keen to develop their skills in automation, data analysis, and project governance within a large, dynamic enterprise. You thrive on learning new technologies and have the adaptability to evolve with the role as the organisation continues its digital transformation. **Not a perfect fit?** Worried that you don’t meet all the desired criteria exactly? At Vodafone we are passionate about empowering people and creating a workplace where everyone can thrive, whatever their personal or professional background. If you’re excited about this role but your experience doesn’t align exactly with every part of the job description, we encourage you to still apply — you may be the right candidate for this role or another opportunity. **What’s in it for you?** * The flexibility to work how you perform best, with hybrid ways of working and real focus on work\-life balance * A competitive package including salary, bonus and a strong pension to support you now and long\-term * ''Generous'' flexible benefits allowance, plus access to comprehensive health and wellbeing support * Access to our on\-site wellness centre and gym, alongside a wider wellbeing programme * Free mobile plan, device offers and exclusive discounts for you, your family and friends * Market\-leading family policies and flexibility to support you at every stage of life * Real opportunities for career growth through learning, development and internal progression **Who We Are** We are a leading international Telco, serving millions of customers. At Vodafone, we believe that connectivity is a force for good. If we use it for the things that really matter, it can improve people’s lives and the world around us. Through our technology, we empower people — connecting everyone regardless of who they are or where they live — and we protect the planet, whilst helping our customers do the same. Belonging at Vodafone isn’t a concept; it’s lived, breathed, and cultivated through everything we do. You’ll be part of a global and diverse community with many different minds, abilities, backgrounds and cultures. We’re committed to increasing diversity, ensuring equal representation, and making Vodafone a place where everyone feels safe, valued and included. Vodafone is committed to supporting candidates with disabilities during the recruitment process. If you require any adjustments, please let us know. **Together we can.**

title: Part-Time Mobile Patrol Security Officer
description: **PART\-TIME MOBILE PATROL SECURITY OFFICER** Weekend Day Shifts (Saturday \& Sunday) **Dublin, Kildare, Laois \& Offaly** CSC Covert Security Consultants Limited is an established Irish total security solutions provider, delivering manned guarding, mobile patrol, alarm response and electronic security services nationwide since 2007\. We are currently recruiting a reliable, professional Mobile Patrol Security Officer to join our team on a part\-time basis, covering Saturday and Sunday day shifts across the Dublin, Kildare, Laois and Offaly region. **The Role** * Carrying out scheduled mobile patrols of client sites across the region * Conducting site checks, lock\-ups / unlocks and key\-holding duties * Responding to alarm activations and reporting incidents promptly * Completing accurate patrol logs and reports * Acting as a professional, visible representation of CSC and our clients **What We’re Looking For** * A valid PSA licence (Security Officer / Door Supervisor) – essential * A full, clean Irish driving licence – ***essential*** * Strong reliability, punctuality and attention to detail * Good written and verbal communication skills * The ability to work independently and use initiative * Previous mobile patrol or security experience an advantage **What We Offer** * €16\.10 per hour – above the industry Employment Regulation Order (ERO) minimum rate * Consistent weekend day\-shift hours, ideal for supplementing income or fitting around other commitments * Full support from an experienced operations team * The opportunity to join a growing, professional Irish security company **How to Apply** To apply, send your CV to lloyd@cscsecurity.ie or call 0818 273 274\. **Please note: you must be currently living in Ireland and hold a valid right to work in Ireland to apply. You must also hold a current PSA licence. Applications from candidates not resident in Ireland cannot be considered.** Pay: €16\.10 per hour Work Location: In person

title: Performance Tester
description: **Job Description – Performance Tester (JMeter / BlazeMeter)** We are looking for an experienced **Performance Tester** with strong expertise in performance testing tools like **JMeter and BlazeMeter** . The ideal candidate should have hands\-on experience in designing, executing, and analyzing performance test scenarios for web, API, and enterprise applications. **Key Responsibilities:** * Design and execute performance test plans, test scenarios, and scripts based on business and technical requirements. * Develop and maintain performance test scripts using **Apache JMeter** and **BlazeMeter** . * Perform different types of performance testing including load, stress, spike, endurance, and scalability testing. * Analyze performance test results and identify bottlenecks across applications, APIs, databases, and infrastructure. * Generate performance reports with key metrics, findings, and recommendations. * Collaborate with development, QA, DevOps, and infrastructure teams to troubleshoot performance issues. * Monitor application behavior during performance tests and provide tuning recommendations. * Integrate performance testing into CI/CD pipelines where required. **Required Skills:** * Strong hands\-on experience in **Performance Testing** . * Expertise in **JMeter** scripting, execution, and result analysis. * Experience with **BlazeMeter** for performance test execution and reporting. * Strong understanding of HTTP/HTTPS protocols, REST APIs, and web application performance testing. * Experience with performance monitoring and analysis tools. * Good knowledge of SQL and database performance analysis. * Understanding of Agile methodology and defect management processes. * Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills. **Good to Have:** * Experience with cloud\-based performance testing environments. * Knowledge of CI/CD tools (Jenkins/Git\-based pipelines). * Experience with Java scripting or Groovy for JMeter enhancements. * Experience testing large\-scale enterprise applications.

title: Power Systems Engineer
description: At **ABB**, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world. **This position reports to:** ELSE Cluster Manager North Europe \_\_ **Your role and responsibilities** In this role you will work as a power engineer on a range of exciting projects for global clients in a rewarding team environment, developing technical and solution development skills to develop into an industry\-recognized consulting engineer. The position would suit a practical and adaptable individual who wishes to work in a progressive, innovative electrical consultancy. We are seeking experienced Power Systems Engineers who are eager to deepen their technical expertise and contribute to complex engineering projects for global clients. This role is ideal for a practical, adaptable, and technically driven engineer who wants to grow into an industry\-recognized consulting specialist within a progressive and innovative electrical consultancy environment. The work model for the role is: \#hybrid, \#Li\-hybrid Driven by strong business growth and increasing customer demand, we are expanding our team and recruiting multiple professionals for this role across the listed countries. You will be mainly accountable for: * Providing technical input and leadership on a variety of complex projects. * Working closely with clients to understand their technical and business requirements and translating them into practical engineering solutions. * Ensuring project outputs meet client expectations, quality standards, timeframes, and budget requirements. * Conducting in\-depth power systems analysis using industry\-leading methodologies and software tools. * Preparing detailed technical and non\-technical reports, presenting clear recommendations and actionable insights. * Attending client meetings remotely, building trusted relationships and managing expectations through effective communication and responsiveness. * Tracking and reporting on project performance and client satisfaction. * Mentoring, coaching and developing more junior engineers. Online training to be delivered where applicable. **Typical projects include:** * Power system studies include load flow, short circuit, protection coordination and system resilience assessments. * Electrical safety and power quality studies, including arc flash, harmonics and earthing assessments. * Grid connection and renewable energy integration projects, covering stability, harmonics and compliance analyses. * Reliability assessments and failure investigations for critical electrical infrastructure. * Software\-based engineering solutions and emerging technology evaluations. * Supporting wind energy projects from design through installation and commissioning. **Qualifications for the role** * You are highly skilled in power system studies, including short circuit, protection coordination and arc flash analysis, and enjoy working on complex electrical infrastructure projects. * You have 3\+ years of experience in power systems engineering, applying technical expertise to deliver reliable, safe and efficient electrical solutions. * You possess advanced knowledge of industry\-leading power system analysis tools such as SKM, EasyPower, ETAP or similar. * You have proven experience in protection system design, settings development and coordination studies, with exposure to transformer and bus differential protection, REF and directional protection schemes. * You have experience performing electrical calculations and developing designs in accordance with relevant IEC standards. * Knowledge of power quality, harmonics and renewable energy integration studies would be an advantage. * You are passionate about solving technical challenges, collaborating with clients and stakeholders, and continuously expanding your expertise. * Degree in Electrical Engineering or a related field. * You are at ease communicating in English and can clearly present technical concepts to a wide range of audiences **Compensation Package** This opportunity is open to candidates in multiple countries, with the final work location determined based on the selected candidate. As compensation may vary by location, we are committed to being transparent throughout the hiring process and will share details of the salary range, variable compensation and benefits package with candidates who progress to the first interview stage. The offer will be tailored based on your role location, experience, skills and qualifications, internal market positioning and alignment with ABB internal standards. **Compensation Package** This opportunity is open to candidates in multiple countries, with the final work location determined based on the selected candidate. As compensation may vary by location, we are committed to being transparent throughout the hiring process and will share details of the salary range, variable compensation and benefits package with candidates who progress to the first interview stage. The offer will be tailored based on your role location, experience, skills and qualifications, internal market positioning and alignment with ABB internal standards. Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences. Ready to make an impact? Apply today or visit **https://www.abb.com** to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

title: PowerBI Developer
description: **Power BI Developer (Sql/ PowerBI )** Our client is looking for a Senior Data BI Developer with expertise in PowerBI/ Dax/ SQl, this is a key role within the information services team, fully aligned to the financial leasing business. The role will look closely with their aviation specialists to build out the core report suite. The role is a hybrid work model and is for a top client in a very attractive sector. The role will offer much career growth in the BI space as they develop their BI capabilities. **Major Responsibilities** * Work with Aviation teams to gather and rationalize internal reporting requirements * Develop Power BI reports to meet business requirements while adhering to data standards * Write and maintain complex SQL stored procedures and views * Work with the BI Manager, Data engineers, Project Managers and Reporting team members, as required, on larger projects and data initiatives * Work with 3rd party suppliers and the global Development team * Offer leadership and suggestions on how to best present data sets and insights * Day\-to\-day support for existing BI reports * Attend daily reporting stand ups **Qualifications** * Third level qualification in Computer Science or Programming or equivalent experience * 5\+ years’ SQL experience * 5\+ years’ experience with Power BI and DAX * Experience working in a DevOps environment and commitment to “build for support” philosophy * Demonstrable knowledge of good data modelling principles * Proven ability to elicit, understand and deliver business reporting requirements * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Experience with GIT or other source control versioning tools * Ability to thrive in a dynamic, fast\-paced environment * Sense of urgency with the ability to produce quality deliverables within tight timeframes and manage a continuous pipeline of work * Team player with the ability to work with multi\-cultural, geographically dispersed teams * Knowledge of data science / machine learning is an advantage **Education:** * Third level qualification in computer science or programming or equivalent experience. **Benefits:** * Full benefits Package (Bonus/ Pension and Healthcare and Great work hours) * Hybrid working 3 days on site 2 days from home * Dublin 2 **HOW TO APPLY:** If you are interested in this role – please apply for this role with your updated CV

title: Private Client Investment Management
description: An established investment and wealth management firm is seeking an experienced Investment Manager to join its Wealth Management function. You'll be responsible for: **Investment Solutions** * Partner with advisers and relationship managers to develop tailored investment strategies for high\-net\-worth individuals, family offices, corporate clients and institutional investors. * Support portfolio construction, strategic asset allocation and ongoing investment recommendations throughout the client lifecycle. * Provide technical expertise on investment solutions while ensuring strong risk management principles. **Market Insights** * Produce regular market commentary, investment updates and thematic research. * Translate complex market developments into practical insights for advisers and clients. * Deliver presentations and communicate investment ideas clearly across internal and external audiences. * Act as a trusted investment specialist for the advisory business. **Investment Innovation** * Work alongside investment specialists and portfolio teams to identify new investment opportunities and product solutions. * Help develop innovative ways to implement investment views using a broad range of instruments. * Contribute to strategic projects aimed at enhancing the firm's advisory offering. **Qualifications** * Degree in Finance, Economics, Investment Management or a related discipline (postgraduate qualification desirable). * Professional qualifications such as CFA, QFA, CAIA or equivalent are highly regarded. **Required Skills** * 5–10 years' experience within investment strategy, portfolio management, wealth management or a related investment role. * Strong understanding of portfolio construction, asset allocation and financial markets. * Experience communicating investment ideas to both technical and non\-technical audiences. * Excellent analytical and quantitative skills. * Advanced Microsoft Excel skills; experience with Bloomberg and investment analytics platforms is advantageous. * Practical knowledge of Python or other programming languages would be beneficial. **Preferred Skills** * Strong commercial awareness and strategic thinking. * Excellent communication and presentation skills. * A collaborative approach with the ability to influence multiple stakeholders. * Curiosity and a proactive mindset for identifying investment opportunities. * Strong attention to detail and analytical capability. * High ethical standards and professionalism. * Ability to build lasting relationships with advisers, clients and internal teams. **If you don't meet all the criteria, but have experience in wealth advisory, portfolio management, investment strategy, or related fields, please contact me at caoimhe.mccoy@markssattin.com.**

title: Project Engineer - Water Networks
description: **The Opportunity** Are you a talented **Project Engineer** with a passion for water network design? We have an exciting opportunity for a highly skilled and motivated individual to join our team at Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec. You'll play a key role in the planning, design, and delivery of crucial water network projects, helping to shape the future of Ireland's water infrastructure. Your responsibilities will include developing detailed designs for water network infrastructure, including pumping stations and storage facilities. You will also prepare feasibility studies, site assessments, and technical evaluations, as well as create specifications and tender documents for projects. You will coordinate with clients, contractors, and our multidisciplinary teams, and you'll manage and guide others in preparing project drawings and other inputs. Throughout the entire project lifecycle, you will provide expert technical support. **About You** You will have a passion for the water sector and have the following : * A third\-level degree in engineering (Level 8 minimum). * Strong post graduate experience in water network design. * Strong professional and technical knowledge. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * The ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team. * A keen eye for detail and strong problem\-solving skills. * The ability to manage deadlines and juggle multiple tasks effectively. * Proficiency in industry\-standard software. **Why Join Us?** * Competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits package. * Access to excellent professional growth and development opportunities. * A collaborative and supportive work environment. * Flexibility to be based in our Dublin, Galway, Cork, or Castlebar office. * Work on impactful projects that are making a real difference across Ireland. If you're ready to take on a challenging and rewarding role, we'd love to hear from you. **Interested? Apply now to join our dynamic team and help us build a better future.** **About Stantec** Whether you’re starting out on your engineering career or are looking for a fresh challenge, Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, is a great career move. Based in Dublin, Galway, Castlebar and Cork, we are actively recruiting for team players with experience in any of the following areas – water and wastewater treatment process and network design, integrated network management, structural design, site development, road and bridge engineering, project management and administration. You’ll be working for a dynamic company with an excellent reputation in the industry. Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, offers a great working environment amongst friendly professionals with an emphasis on teamwork. It’s a company where your ideas and opinions will be valued and your efforts recognised and rewarded. We have been awarded the silver Investors in Diversity accreditation by the Irish Centre for Diversity for our commitment to quality, diversity and inclusion (EDI) in the workplace.

title: Qualified Early Years Educator
description: See our new website \-\-\-\> https://littlefootsteps.netlify.app/ Join our growing team as we seek to expand our footprint in North County Dublin. Little Footsteps Creche \& Montessori is currently looking for a qualified early years educator to join our expanding team. ***Applicant Requirements :*** * QQI Childcare Qualification * Ability to look after children in a loving \& caring manner * Ability to work as part of a team * Good working knowledge of the Aistear \& Siolta curriculum * Ability to implement quality early childhood curriculum \& creative skills to develop age appropriate activities * Understand \& follow TUSLA regulations * Strong work ethic * Subject to Garda Clearance * Please apply from within Ireland only * Pay based on QQI level. Our team consists of 8 staff and we cater for 32 children overall within 3 different rooms \- wobbler, toddler and Montessori. We are seeking another member for our expanding team. We look forward to having you join the team! Job Types: Permanent, Graduate Pay: €17\.50 per hour Work Location: In person

title: Quantity Surveyor
description: We're expanding our team and looking for an experienced Quantity Surveyor to support our development team. If you're a self starter, with strong commercial experience, this is your opportunity to make a real impact with a renowned developer in Ireland. The candidate must be able to work from site in Sandyford Dublin a minimum of 3 days per week. There is potential to work from other office locations in Dunboyne Meath or Palmerstown House Estate (Award winning gold course) close to Naas Kildare. **Role Responsibilities** * Prepare tender documentation, evaluate subcontractor proposals, and manage negotiations through to order placement. * Oversee the financial and commercial management of subcontractors, ensuring smooth progression to final account settlement. * Maintain accurate site records and ensure robust document control processes. * Carry out site measurements and record findings diligently. * Contribute to forecasting, target setting, and reconciliation of package values. * Compile valuations and variations with comprehensive supporting details, agreeing adjustments as work advances. * Assist with reconciling proposed payment runs. * Update and submit projected final accounts promptly. * Collaborate with site management to address and challenge commercial issues effectively. * Drive the implementation of the agreed commercial strategy. * Take part in contract value reviews, maintaining an objective perspective. * Identify potential risks and opportunities, providing informed assessments. * Spearhead the project cost tracking process ensuring budgets and actual spend are aligned and maintained. * Build knowledge of insurance requirements and warranty obligations. * Support cash flow management activities to maintain healthy financial performance. * Assist with tendering processes when required. * Supervise and mentor Junior Quantity Surveyors, ensuring quality and accuracy in their work. * Ensure all site records and control documents are properly maintained. * Communicate clearly and effectively, both in writing and verbally. * Make full use of digital tools and technology platforms. * Maintain proficiency in Standard Methods of Measurement such as ARM5 where applicable depending on the package * Demonstrate strong commercial awareness and accountability. * Apply effective time management, organisational, interpersonal, and negotiation skills. **Qualifications and Requirements** * Minimum 4 years experience working as a quantity surveyor in Ireland, ideally with a developer or main contractor. * Excellent English level minimum C1 * Must be able to work from site in Sandyford Dublin a minimum of 3 days per week, with potential to work from locations in Dunboyne Meath or Palmerstown House Estate close to Naas Kildare * Degree\-level education and progression towards relevant professional accreditation. * Proficient in Microsoft excel and measurement tools such as Bluebeam / Cubit etc * Strong ability for cost analysis and separating value and risk from noise. * Skilled in negotiation and always driving for best value for Comer Group. * Able to manage multiple workflows simultaneously
